supplements.name: Zinc

supplements.name: Bacopa Monnieri

supplements.name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)

supplements.shortDesc: Fuels mitochondria, protects heart and cells.

supplements.name: Berberine

supplements.shortDesc: Blood sugar control, gut health, and longevity.

forms.name: Berberine HCl
forms.notes: Take with meals for best effect.

supplements.mechanism: Activates AMPK (the 'metabolic master switch'), inhibits glucose absorption, and modulates gut microbiota composition.

supplements.name: Collagen Peptides
supplements.summary: Hydrolyzed collagen peptides support skin elasticity, joint cartilage repair, and connective tissue health.
supplements.shortDesc: Skin elasticity, joint repair, and connective tissue.
supplements.mechanism: Supplies glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline to stimulate endogenous collagen synthesis in fibroblasts.
supplements.withFood: With Vitamin C to enhance synthesis

supplements.name: Vitamin C
supplements.summary: A powerful antioxidant and cofactor for collagen synthesis, immune function, and iron absorption.
supplements.shortDesc: Antioxidant powerhouse and immune defender.
supplements.mechanism: Donates electrons as a reducing agent; essential cofactor for prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase enzymes in collagen synthesis.

supplements.name: NMN (Nicotinamide Mononucleotide)
supplements.summary: A direct precursor to NAD+ that bypasses rate-limiting steps in the NAD+ biosynthesis pathway, supporting mitochondrial function, DNA repair, SIRT1 activation, and cellular aging reversal.
supplements.shortDesc: Direct NAD+ precursor for mitochondrial health, DNA repair, and anti-aging.
supplements.mechanism: Converted to NAD+ via NMNAT enzymes in cells; NAD+ activates sirtuins (SIRT1–7) and PARPs for DNA repair; substrate for CD38 (consumed, hence apigenin synergy); fuels electron transport chain.
supplements.bestTime: Morning on empty stomach or pre-workout
supplements.withFood: Can take fasted or with meals
interactions: Chemotherapy (theoretical, NAD fuels DNA repair in tumors)

supplements.name: Resveratrol
supplements.summary: A polyphenol found in red grapes that activates sirtuin longevity pathways and has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Activates longevity genes, powerful antioxidant.
supplements.mechanism: Activates SIRT1 deacetylase; inhibits NF-κB inflammatory pathway; mimics caloric restriction at cellular level.

supplements.name: Probiotics
supplements.summary: Beneficial bacteria that colonize the gut, improving microbiome diversity, digestion, immunity, and the gut-brain axis.
supplements.shortDesc: Restores gut flora, boosts immunity, supports mood.
supplements.mechanism: Compete with pathogenic bacteria, produce short-chain fatty acids, modulate intestinal immune cells, and signal via vagus nerve.

supplements.name: Beta-Alanine
supplements.summary: Beta-alanine buffers lactic acid in muscles, delaying fatigue during high-intensity exercise and extending endurance capacity.
supplements.shortDesc: Buffers muscle acid, delays fatigue, boosts endurance.
supplements.mechanism: Combines with histidine to form carnosine in muscle, acting as an intracellular pH buffer during anaerobic exercise.

supplements.name: L-Citrulline
supplements.summary: A superior nitric oxide booster that bypasses first-pass liver metabolism, producing more sustained NO than arginine.
supplements.shortDesc: Better than arginine for nitric oxide and performance.

forms.name: L-Citrulline (pure)
forms.notes: Preferred over citrulline malate for pure NO.

forms.name: Citrulline Malate 2:1
forms.notes: Malate also reduces fatigue; research standard.

supplements.mechanism: Bypasses hepatic arginase degradation; converted to arginine in kidney providing sustained NOS substrate.

supplements.name: Melatonin
supplements.summary: The body's primary sleep-onset hormone, melatonin regulates circadian rhythm and is particularly effective for jet lag and shift work.
supplements.shortDesc: Regulates sleep onset and circadian rhythm.
supplements.mechanism: Binds MT1 and MT2 receptors in the SCN, signalling darkness to the brain and initiating sleep onset cascade.
sideEffects: Potential hormonal disruption at high doses

supplements.name: 5-HTP
supplements.summary: A direct precursor to serotonin, 5-HTP supports mood, reduces anxiety, improves sleep quality, and may curb appetite.
supplements.shortDesc: Boosts serotonin for mood, sleep, and calm.
supplements.mechanism: Directly decarboxylated to serotonin in the brain; bypasses tryptophan hydroxylase rate-limiting step.
supplements.withFood: With food reduces GI side effects
sideEffects: Serotonin syndrome risk if combined with antidepressants

supplements.name: TUDCA
supplements.summary: Tauroursodeoxycholic acid is a bile acid that protects liver cells, reduces endoplasmic reticulum stress, and supports mitochondrial integrity.
supplements.shortDesc: Protects liver cells, reduces cellular stress.
supplements.mechanism: Activates the GLP-1 receptor, modulates bile acid signalling, reduces hepatocyte apoptosis, and inhibits inflammatory cascades.
supplements.withFood: Yes — with food for best absorption

supplements.name: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine)
supplements.summary: A cysteine derivative and glutathione precursor with mucolytic, hepatoprotective, and antioxidant properties, used for acetaminophen overdose, respiratory conditions, and OCD.
supplements.shortDesc: Glutathione precursor and mucolytic for liver detox, respiratory, and OCD relief.
supplements.mechanism: Converted to cysteine → glutathione (via gamma-glutamylcysteine synthetase); disrupts disulfide bonds in mucus; inhibits NF-kB; glutamate modulator (reduces glutamate excess in OCD); reduces NMDA excitotoxicity.
supplements.withFood: With food to reduce GI upset
sideEffects: Possible worsening of asthma at very high doses

supplements.name: Tongkat Ali
supplements.summary: A Southeast Asian herb with robust evidence for raising free testosterone by reducing SHBG, improving libido, mood, and body composition.
supplements.shortDesc: Raises free testosterone, boosts libido and vitality.

supplements.mechanism: Quassinoids inhibit SHBG binding to testosterone, increasing free T; also inhibits aromatase and stimulates Leydig cell activity.

supplements.name: Fadogia Agrestis
supplements.summary: An African shrub extract traditionally used for virility, with preliminary evidence suggesting LH-mediated testosterone elevation.
supplements.shortDesc: Stimulates LH release to support testosterone.

forms.name: Root extract
forms.notes: Limited human trials — cycle use is advised.

supplements.mechanism: Believed to stimulate luteinizing hormone (LH) from the pituitary, signalling Leydig cells to produce more testosterone.
sideEffects: Potential testicular toxicity at high doses (animal data)

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7)

supplements.shortDesc: Strengthens hair, nails, and supports skin health.

forms.name: D-Biotin
forms.notes: The natural active form; look for D-Biotin not DL-Biotin.

supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for carboxylase enzymes involved in fatty acid synthesis and amino acid metabolism critical for keratin production.

interactions: Can skew lab results — inform your doctor

supplements.name: Hyaluronic Acid

forms.name: Low molecular weight HA

supplements.mechanism: Retains water in extracellular matrix of skin and cartilage; stimulates chondrocyte synthesis and fibroblast proliferation.

supplements.name: Phosphatidylserine

forms.name: PS (Sunflower-derived)

supplements.mechanism: Integrates into neuronal membranes, supporting acetylcholine release, glucose uptake, and dopamine/serotonin synthesis in neurons.

supplements.name: Alpha Lipoic Acid

supplements.shortDesc: Universal antioxidant, insulin sensitizer, nerve protector.

forms.name: R-ALA

supplements.name: Quercetin

forms.name: Quercetin Phytosome (Quercefit)

supplements.bestTime: With meals; with zinc for antiviral effect

supplements.name: Vitamin B12

forms.name: Methylcobalamin

forms.name: Cyanocobalamin

supplements.name: Maca Root

supplements.name: Vitamin B5 (Pantothenic Acid)

supplements.name: Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine)

supplements.name: Vitamin B9 (Folate)

supplements.name: Vitamin B12 (Methylcobalamin)

forms.name: Hydroxocobalamin

supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for methionine synthase (methylation) and methylmalonyl-CoA mutase (mitochondrial metabolism).

supplements.name: Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid)

supplements.name: Vitamin E (Tocopherol)

supplements.name: Vitamin K1 (Phylloquinone)
supplements.summary: Plant-derived K1 is essential for blood coagulation factor activation.

supplements.name: Vitamin K2 (MK-4)

supplements.shortDesc: Bone mineralization and rapid K2 activity.

forms.name: MK-4 supplement
forms.notes: Used at high therapeutic doses in Japan.

supplements.mechanism: Converted from K1 in tissues; activates carboxylase enzymes; shorter half-life than MK-7.

supplements.name: Calcium (Carbonate)

supplements.shortDesc: Bone density and muscle contraction.

forms.name: Calcium Carbonate
forms.notes: Take with meals; requires stomach acid.

forms.name: Calcium Hydroxyapatite
forms.notes: Includes collagen matrix; superior for bone density.

supplements.mechanism: Activates myosin-actin cross-bridge cycling; regulates calmodulin and PKC signaling cascades.
supplements.withFood: With meals for carbonate; any time for citrate

supplements.name: Calcium Citrate

supplements.shortDesc: Superior absorbed calcium for bone health.

forms.name: Calcium Citrate
forms.notes: No acid required; take any time.

supplements.mechanism: Dissociates in neutral pH; citrate chelate enhances intestinal transport via transcellular pathway.

supplements.name: Magnesium Glycinate
supplements.summary: Chelated magnesium bound to glycine — the gold standard for sleep, anxiety, and muscle recovery.
supplements.shortDesc: Best absorbed magnesium for sleep and relaxation.

supplements.name: Magnesium Citrate
supplements.summary: A highly bioavailable magnesium salt with mild osmotic laxative properties, popular for general use.
supplements.shortDesc: High-absorption magnesium; supports regularity.
supplements.mechanism: Citrate complexation increases solubility and absorption; osmotic effect draws water into colon.

supplements.name: Magnesium L-Threonate

supplements.shortDesc: Brain-penetrating magnesium for cognitive enhancement, memory, and sleep.
supplements.mechanism: Threonate carrier actively transports magnesium across BBB via sodium-dependent transporter; increases brain Mg²⁺ → enhances NMDA receptor function → improves synaptic plasticity → LTP and memory.

supplements.name: Magnesium Oxide
supplements.summary: A high-elemental-content magnesium form with poor absorption, mainly used for antacid and laxative purposes.
supplements.shortDesc: High elemental Mg; low absorption, good for heartburn.

forms.name: Magnesium Oxide
forms.notes: Cheap; not ideal for systemic deficiency.

supplements.mechanism: Neutralizes stomach acid; poorly absorbed through intestinal wall due to low solubility.

supplements.name: Magnesium Malate
supplements.summary: Magnesium bound to malic acid — supports energy production in the Krebs cycle and reduces fibromyalgia pain.
supplements.shortDesc: Energy-boosting magnesium; fibromyalgia support.

forms.name: Magnesium Malate
forms.notes: Malate fuels TCA cycle alongside Mg.

supplements.mechanism: Malate is a TCA cycle intermediate; magnesium activates ATP synthase improving mitochondrial energy output.

supplements.name: Magnesium Taurate
supplements.summary: Magnesium chelated with taurine, with synergistic benefits for cardiovascular health and blood pressure.
supplements.shortDesc: Heart-focused magnesium with taurine synergy.

forms.name: Magnesium Taurate
forms.notes: Taurine and Mg share cardiac protective effects.

supplements.mechanism: Taurine modulates calcium channels; magnesium inhibits excessive NMDA activity stabilizing cardiac cells.

supplements.name: Magnesium Chloride
supplements.summary: A highly soluble magnesium salt used orally and transdermally for rapid magnesium repletion.
supplements.shortDesc: Fast-absorbing magnesium for topical and oral use.
supplements.mechanism: High solubility enables fast intestinal absorption; transdermal route bypasses GI system.

supplements.name: Magnesium Sulfate (Epsom Salt)
supplements.summary: Classically used as an Epsom salt bath for sore muscles; also used IV in clinical medicine.
supplements.shortDesc: Epsom salt: muscle soak and clinical magnesium.

forms.name: Epsom Salt bath
forms.notes: Dissolve 2 cups in warm bath; soak 20 min.

forms.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV
forms.notes: Used in hospitals for eclampsia and arrhythmias.

supplements.mechanism: Topical evidence is debated; IV MgSO4 directly raises plasma Mg2+ competing with calcium at synapses.

supplements.name: Zinc Picolinate
supplements.summary: The most bioavailable chelated zinc form, bound to picolinic acid for superior intestinal absorption.
supplements.shortDesc: Highly absorbed zinc for immunity and testosterone.
supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for 300+ enzymes; zinc finger proteins regulate transcription; supports T-cell development.

supplements.name: Zinc Gluconate
supplements.summary: A moderately bioavailable zinc salt widely used in cold lozenges and general supplementation.
supplements.shortDesc: Standard zinc for immunity and cold relief.
supplements.mechanism: Releases Zn2+ ions that inhibit ICAM-1 rhinovirus binding in nasal passages.

supplements.name: Zinc Bisglycinate
supplements.summary: Double-chelated zinc with two glycine molecules for maximum absorption and minimal GI irritation.
supplements.shortDesc: Best-tolerated zinc chelate for sensitive stomachs.
supplements.mechanism: Glycinate carrier facilitates active transport across enterocytes with minimal competition from other minerals.

supplements.name: Iron (Ferrous Bisglycinate)

supplements.shortDesc: Gentlest, best-absorbed iron for anemia.

forms.name: Ferrous Sulfate

supplements.mechanism: Absorbed via DMT-1 transporter; heme iron bypasses regulation; ferritin stores; hepcidin regulates.
supplements.bestTime: On empty stomach for absorption; with food if GI upset

supplements.name: Selenium (Selenomethionine)
supplements.summary: An essential trace mineral bound to methionine for optimal absorption; supports thyroid and antioxidant defense.
supplements.shortDesc: Thyroid function and antioxidant glutathione support.

forms.name: Sodium Selenite

supplements.mechanism: Incorporated into selenoproteins (GPx1-4, TrxR1, DIO1/2) protecting against oxidative damage.
sideEffects: Selenosis above 400 mcg: hair loss, nail brittleness, garlic breath

supplements.name: Iodine

supplements.shortDesc: Thyroid hormone production and metabolism.

forms.name: Lugol's Solution
forms.notes: Mix of iodine and potassium iodide.

forms.name: Nascent Iodine
forms.notes: Atomic iodine in ethanol; highly bioactive claim.

supplements.mechanism: Iodide oxidized by thyroid peroxidase to iodinate tyrosine residues on thyroglobulin forming T3/T4.

supplements.name: Copper (Bisglycinate)
supplements.summary: An often-overlooked trace mineral essential for iron metabolism, collagen synthesis, and energy production.
supplements.shortDesc: Iron partner; collagen and energy cofactor.

forms.name: Copper Picolinate
forms.notes: Picolinate chelate similar to zinc form.

supplements.mechanism: Cuproenzymes include ceruloplasmin (Fe3+ oxidation), CCO (ATP), Cu/Zn-SOD (antioxidant), and lysyl oxidase (collagen).
supplements.withFood: With food; separate from zinc by 2h

supplements.name: Manganese
supplements.summary: A trace mineral critical for bone formation, wound healing, and mitochondrial antioxidant defense (Mn-SOD).
supplements.shortDesc: Bone, wound healing, and mitochondrial antioxidant.
supplements.mechanism: Activates arginase, glucokinase, and mitochondrial Mn-SOD; cofactor for glycosyltransferases in bone matrix.

supplements.name: Chromium Picolinate
supplements.summary: Enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake; chromium picolinate is the most studied bioavailable form.
supplements.shortDesc: Insulin sensitivity and blood sugar regulation.
supplements.mechanism: Potentiates insulin receptor tyrosine kinase activity via chromodulin (low molecular weight chromium-binding substance).
sideEffects: Theoretical DNA damage at very high doses (picolinate form)

supplements.name: Molybdenum
supplements.summary: A trace mineral required for enzyme cofactors involved in sulfur metabolism and uric acid production.
supplements.shortDesc: Sulfur detoxification and enzyme cofactor.
supplements.mechanism: Molybdopterin cofactor activates sulfite oxidase, xanthine oxidase, and aldehyde oxidase.
sideEffects: Interferes with copper metabolism at high doses

supplements.name: Boron
supplements.summary: A trace mineral that enhances the effects of estrogen, testosterone, and vitamin D while supporting bone health.
supplements.shortDesc: Amplifies vitamin D and steroid hormone activity.
supplements.mechanism: Inhibits 24-hydroxylase extending vitamin D half-life; increases SHBG-independent testosterone fraction.
sideEffects: Reproductive toxicity at very high doses

supplements.name: Silica (Silicon)
supplements.summary: Silicon supports collagen cross-linking, bone matrix formation, and connective tissue integrity.
supplements.shortDesc: Collagen and connective tissue structural support.

forms.name: Silicon Dioxide
forms.notes: Used as anti-caking agent; poor absorption.

supplements.mechanism: Cross-links hydroxylysine residues in collagen precursors; stimulates osteoblast differentiation.
sideEffects: Renal silicosis at extreme long-term excess

supplements.name: Potassium (Citrate)
supplements.summary: The primary intracellular cation; potassium citrate alkalizes urine and supports blood pressure regulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Blood pressure and electrolyte balance.

dosage.upperLimit: No established UL (caution with kidney disease)

supplements.mechanism: Na+/K+-ATPase maintains 30x intracellular K+ gradient; citrate raises urinary pH inhibiting calcium oxalate crystals.
supplements.withFood: With food to avoid GI upset
sideEffects: Hyperkalemia (dangerous with kidney disease or ACE inhibitors)

supplements.name: Sodium
supplements.summary: The primary extracellular electrolyte governing fluid balance, nerve transmission, and muscle contraction.
supplements.shortDesc: Hydration, nerve function, and fluid balance.

forms.name: Himalayan Pink Salt
forms.notes: Contains 84 trace minerals at negligible levels.

forms.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
forms.notes: 1 tsp = ~1000 mg sodium; buffers lactic acid.

supplements.mechanism: Extracellular Na+ drives action potentials; Na+/K+ pump maintains gradients; osmolality regulates ADH.

supplements.name: Phosphorus
supplements.summary: The second-most abundant mineral in the body; essential for ATP, DNA/RNA, and bone mineralization.
supplements.shortDesc: ATP, DNA, and bone structure.
supplements.mechanism: Phosphorylation drives ATP synthesis; hydroxyapatite (Ca10(PO4)6(OH)2) is bone mineral phase.
sideEffects: Calcium depletion at very high doses

supplements.name: Fluoride
supplements.summary: A trace mineral that incorporates into tooth enamel as fluorapatite, making it more resistant to acid erosion.
supplements.shortDesc: Dental cavity prevention.
supplements.mechanism: F- substitutes OH- in hydroxyapatite forming fluorapatite; more acid-resistant crystal structure.
sideEffects: Skeletal fluorosis at very high doses

supplements.name: Vanadium
supplements.summary: A trace mineral with insulin-mimetic properties used experimentally for blood sugar regulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Insulin-mimetic trace mineral for blood sugar.
supplements.mechanism: Vanadate inhibits phosphotyrosine phosphatases potentiating insulin receptor signaling.
supplements.withFood: With food to reduce GI upset
sideEffects: Potential kidney toxicity at high doses

supplements.name: Turmeric (BCM-95 Curcumin)
supplements.summary: BCM-95 combines curcuminoids with turmeric essential oils for 7x superior bioavailability over standard curcumin.
supplements.shortDesc: Enhanced-absorption curcumin for inflammation.

forms.name: Curcumin + Piperine (Bioperine)
forms.notes: Black pepper extract; most affordable enhanced.

supplements.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB, COX-2, and LOX inflammatory enzymes; activates Nrf2 antioxidant response; modulates BDNF.

supplements.name: Boswellia Serrata
supplements.summary: Boswellic acids, particularly AKBA, potently inhibit 5-LOX without the GI side effects of NSAIDs.
supplements.shortDesc: Powerful anti-inflammatory; joint and gut support.
supplements.mechanism: AKBA non-competitively inhibits 5-lipoxygenase reducing leukotriene synthesis and joint inflammation.

supplements.name: Ginger Extract
supplements.summary: Gingerols and shogaols inhibit prostaglandin synthesis and support digestion, nausea, and inflammation.
supplements.shortDesc: Anti-nausea, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid.
supplements.mechanism: Gingerols inhibit COX-1/2 and 5-LOX; 6-shogaol antagonizes 5-HT3 reducing nausea signaling.

supplements.name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)
supplements.summary: Ocimum sanctum reduces cortisol, blood glucose, and anxiety through multiple adaptogenic mechanisms.
supplements.shortDesc: Adaptogen for stress, blood sugar, and immunity.
supplements.mechanism: Eugenol inhibits COX enzymes; ursolic acid activates AMPK; adaptogenic compounds modulate HPA axis.
sideEffects: May lower blood sugar too much in diabetics

supplements.name: Siberian Ginseng (Eleuthero)
supplements.summary: Eleutherococcus senticosus contains eleutherosides that enhance physical endurance and stress resistance.
supplements.shortDesc: Endurance and stress-resistance adaptogen.
supplements.mechanism: Eleutherosides modulate corticoadrenal secretion; adaptogens normalize stress response via HPA/SAM axes.

supplements.name: Panax Ginseng
supplements.summary: Korean red ginseng with ginsenosides that enhance energy, libido, cognitive function, and immune modulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Classic energy and vitality adaptogen.

forms.name: Korean Red Ginseng (KRG)
forms.notes: Steamed root; more potent ginsenoside profile.

supplements.mechanism: Ginsenosides Rg1/Rb1 modulate nAChR; Rb1 activates eNOS; Rg3 inhibits cancer cell proliferation.

supplements.name: American Ginseng
supplements.summary: Panax quinquefolius with a calmer, more sedative ginsenoside profile than Korean ginseng.
supplements.shortDesc: Calming ginseng for blood sugar and immune support.
supplements.mechanism: Higher Rb1:Rg1 ratio produces more sedating, less stimulating effect; modulates pancreatic insulin release.

supplements.name: Schisandra Chinensis
supplements.summary: The five-flavor berry is a classical TCM adaptogen supporting liver detox, endurance, and mental clarity.
supplements.shortDesc: Liver-protective adaptogen with cognitive benefits.
supplements.mechanism: Schisandrins activate Nrf2 pathway inducing phase II detox enzymes; adaptogenic via adrenal modulation.

supplements.name: Gotu Kola
supplements.summary: Centella asiatica contains triterpenoids that stimulate collagen synthesis and improve cognitive function.
supplements.shortDesc: Collagen synthesis and brain circulation support.
supplements.mechanism: Asiaticoside stimulates type I collagen gene expression; madecassoside inhibits Akt increasing BDNF.

supplements.name: Milk Thistle (Silymarin)
supplements.summary: Silymarin from Silybum marianum is the gold-standard liver-protective botanical, inhibiting toxin uptake.
supplements.shortDesc: Liver protection and detoxification support.
supplements.mechanism: Silybin competitively inhibits hepatic toxin transport proteins; scavenges free radicals; stimulates ribosomal RNA.

supplements.name: Valerian Root
supplements.summary: Valerenic acid modulates GABA-A receptors promoting sleep onset and quality without creating dependency.
supplements.shortDesc: Natural sleep aid via GABA modulation.
supplements.mechanism: Valerenic acid inhibits GABA transaminase degradation and binds GABA-A receptors allosterically.
sideEffects: Liver concerns at very high doses

supplements.name: Passionflower (Passiflora)
supplements.summary: Chrysin and vitexin in passionflower promote GABAergic relaxation and reduce generalized anxiety.
supplements.shortDesc: Gentle anxiolytic and sleep-quality enhancer.
supplements.mechanism: Chrysin binds central GABA-A receptors like low-efficacy benzodiazepine; vitexin modulates 5-HT2A.

supplements.name: Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis)
supplements.summary: A calming herb from the mint family that reduces GABA degradation via GABA transaminase inhibition, producing mild sedation, anxiety relief, and improved sleep.
supplements.shortDesc: GABA transaminase inhibitor for anxiety, sleep, and cognitive calm.

forms.name: Lemon Balm Extract (Cyracos, standardized)
forms.notes: Standardized for rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

supplements.mechanism: Rosmarinic acid inhibits GABA transaminase (enzyme breaking down GABA); flavonoids modulate serotonergic receptors; antiviral (anti-herpes) rosmarinic acid mechanism.
supplements.bestTime: Evening or 30 min before stressful events
sideEffects: Thyroid inhibition at very high doses (theoretical)

supplements.name: Chamomile (Apigenin)
supplements.summary: Apigenin, chamomile's key flavonoid, binds benzodiazepine receptors providing mild anxiolytic and sleep effects.
supplements.shortDesc: Mild calming herb; benzodiazepine receptor partial agonist.
supplements.mechanism: Apigenin binds GABA-A receptor benzodiazepine site as partial agonist without tolerance induction.

supplements.name: Hawthorn Berry
supplements.summary: Oligomeric proanthocyanidins in hawthorn dilate coronary arteries, improve cardiac output, and reduce palpitations.
supplements.shortDesc: Heart-supportive herb for cardiovascular function.
supplements.mechanism: OPCs inhibit phosphodiesterase increasing intracellular cAMP; flavonoids reduce ACE activity; improve Ca2+ sensitivity.

supplements.name: Elderberry (Sambucus nigra)
supplements.summary: Anthocyanins and flavonoids in elderberry reduce viral replication, shorten cold/flu duration, and support immunity.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune-boosting antiviral berry extract.
supplements.mechanism: Anthocyanins inhibit hemagglutinin spike preventing influenza cell entry; stimulate cytokine production.
supplements.bestTime: At first sign of illness or daily in winter
sideEffects: May overstimulate immune system in autoimmune conditions

supplements.name: Astragalus (Huang Qi)
supplements.summary: Astragalosides activate telomerase extending telomere length; potently immunomodulatory and adaptogenic.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune tonic and telomere-supporting longevity herb.
supplements.mechanism: Astragalosides IV/cycloastragenol activate hTERT (telomerase reverse transcriptase) extending telomeres.
sideEffects: Possible immune overstimulation in autoimmune conditions

supplements.name: Cat's Claw (Una de Gato)
supplements.summary: Pentacyclic oxindole alkaloids in cat's claw modulate immunity and reduce chronic inflammation.
supplements.shortDesc: Anti-inflammatory immune modulator from the Amazon.
supplements.mechanism: POA alkaloids modulate NF-κB pathway; proanthocyanidins inhibit TNF-α and IL-6 production.

supplements.name: Devil's Claw
supplements.summary: Harpagoside from devil's claw root inhibits COX-2 and 5-LOX comparable to some NSAIDs for back pain.
supplements.shortDesc: Natural anti-inflammatory for back and joint pain.
supplements.mechanism: Harpagoside inhibits LPS-stimulated COX-2 and iNOS expression; reduces TNF-α and IL-1β.

supplements.name: Andrographis
supplements.summary: Andrographolide is a potent immunostimulant shown to reduce cold severity and duration in multiple trials.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune-activating herb for cold and flu.
supplements.mechanism: Andrographolide activates NF-κB, increases NK cell and T-cell activity; direct anti-inflammatory via NF-κB inhibition.
supplements.bestTime: At onset of illness or as preventive

supplements.name: Oregano Oil
supplements.summary: Carvacrol and thymol in oregano oil disrupt bacterial and fungal cell membranes with broad antimicrobial activity.
supplements.shortDesc: Potent antimicrobial and antifungal herbal oil.

forms.name: Emulsified Oregano Oil
forms.notes: Mixed with olive oil; easier to take.

supplements.mechanism: Carvacrol disrupts bacterial membrane potential; thymol chelates iron reducing bacterial growth.

supplements.name: Garlic (Allicin/Kyolic)
supplements.summary: Allicin and sulfur compounds in garlic reduce blood pressure, cholesterol, and have broad antimicrobial activity.
supplements.shortDesc: Cardiovascular and antimicrobial herb.
supplements.mechanism: Allicin inhibits cysteine proteases in bacteria; thiosulfinates reduce HMG-CoA reductase; inhibit platelet aggregation.
supplements.withFood: With food to reduce GI irritation

supplements.name: Ginkgo Biloba
supplements.summary: Ginkgo flavonoids and terpenoids improve cerebral blood flow, memory, and tinnitus in multiple RCTs.
supplements.shortDesc: Cerebral circulation and memory herb.
supplements.mechanism: Ginkgolides antagonize PAF (platelet-activating factor); flavonoids scavenge ROS; improves NO-dependent vasodilation.

supplements.name: St. John's Wort
supplements.summary: Hypericin and hyperforin inhibit serotonin/dopamine/norepinephrine reuptake comparably to mild SSRIs for depression.
supplements.shortDesc: Herbal antidepressant via monoamine reuptake inhibition.
supplements.mechanism: Hyperforin inhibits reuptake transporters for serotonin, dopamine, GABA, and glutamate via sodium conductive pathway.

supplements.name: Kava (Piper methysticum)
supplements.summary: Kavalactones modulate GABA-A and NMDA receptors producing anxiolytic effects without cognitive impairment.
supplements.shortDesc: Potent anxiety relief without sedation or dependence.
supplements.mechanism: Methysticin and dihydromethysticin block voltage-gated Na+ channels; bind GABA-A, NMDA receptors; inhibit MAO-B.
supplements.withFood: On empty stomach (traditional); or as tolerated
sideEffects: Liver toxicity risk with non-noble cultivars or heavy use

supplements.name: Tribulus Terrestris
supplements.summary: Protodioscin in tribulus stimulates LH release and may support testosterone in deficient individuals.
supplements.shortDesc: Traditional testosterone and libido herb.
supplements.mechanism: Protodioscin stimulates pituitary LH release; in animal models increases free testosterone; unclear in healthy men.
sideEffects: Kidney damage at very high doses

supplements.name: Horny Goat Weed (Epimedium)
supplements.summary: Icariin is a PDE5 inhibitor supporting erectile function, bone health, and testosterone levels.
supplements.shortDesc: Natural PDE5 inhibitor for libido and bone health.
supplements.mechanism: Icariin inhibits PDE5 increasing cGMP in smooth muscle; stimulates ER-β supporting bone formation; increases testosterone.

supplements.name: Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens)
supplements.summary: Liposterolic extract of saw palmetto inhibits 5-alpha reductase reducing DHT, supporting prostate and hair health.
supplements.shortDesc: 5-alpha reductase inhibitor for prostate and hair.
supplements.mechanism: Inhibits both isoforms of 5α-reductase (I and II); blocks binding of DHT to androgen receptors in prostate.

supplements.name: Pygeum Africanum
supplements.summary: Phytosterols and pentacyclic triterpenoids in pygeum bark reduce prostate inflammation and BPH symptoms.
supplements.shortDesc: African prostate herb reducing inflammation and BPH.

forms.name: Pygeum bark powder
forms.notes: Bark; needs high dose for effect.

supplements.mechanism: Beta-sitosterol inhibits prostatic cell proliferation; atraric acid blocks testosterone receptor activation in prostate.

supplements.name: Stinging Nettle Root
supplements.summary: Nettle root lectins bind SHBG reducing testosterone binding, increasing free testosterone bioavailability.
supplements.shortDesc: Increases free testosterone by binding SHBG.
supplements.mechanism: Root lectin (UDA) binds to SHBG reducing testosterone binding; polysaccharides inhibit prostate cell growth.

supplements.name: Fenugreek
supplements.summary: Steroidal saponins in fenugreek support testosterone, insulin sensitivity, and libido in both sexes.
supplements.shortDesc: Testosterone and libido support; blood sugar aid.
supplements.mechanism: 4-hydroxyisoleucine stimulates insulin secretion; steroidal saponins inhibit aromatase increasing free testosterone.

supplements.name: Cinnamon Extract (Ceylon)
supplements.summary: Ceylon cinnamon's cinnamaldehyde and A-type proanthocyanidins enhance insulin sensitivity without coumarin risk.
supplements.shortDesc: Safe cinnamon for blood sugar and metabolic health.
supplements.mechanism: Cinnamaldehyde activates insulin receptor kinase; inhibits PTP1B increasing insulin sensitivity; antioxidant via NF-κB.
sideEffects: Liver damage (cassia at high doses from coumarin)

supplements.name: Gymnema Sylvestre
supplements.summary: Gymnemic acids block sweet taste receptors and reduce glucose absorption, supporting blood sugar control.
supplements.shortDesc: Blocks sugar cravings and reduces glucose absorption.
supplements.mechanism: Gymnemic acids share structural similarity with glucose, blocking intestinal glucose receptors (SGLT-1) and sweet taste receptors.

supplements.name: Bitter Melon (Momordica charantia)
supplements.summary: Charantin and polypeptide-p in bitter melon mimic insulin activity, supporting blood sugar control.
supplements.shortDesc: Insulin-mimetic plant for blood glucose management.
supplements.mechanism: Polypeptide-p mimics insulin; charantin activates PPAR-α/γ; reduces hepatic glucose production.

supplements.name: L-Glutamine
supplements.summary: The most abundant free amino acid in the body; critical for gut barrier integrity, immunity, and recovery.
supplements.shortDesc: Gut integrity, immunity, and muscle recovery.
supplements.mechanism: Fuel for rapidly dividing enterocytes maintaining tight junctions; substrate for lymphocyte proliferation.
sideEffects: GI upset at very high doses

supplements.name: L-Arginine
supplements.summary: Arginine is the substrate for nitric oxide synthesis, supporting vasodilation, blood pressure, and erectile function.
supplements.shortDesc: Nitric oxide precursor for blood flow and performance.

forms.name: Arginine Alpha-Ketoglutarate (AAKG)
forms.notes: AKG may enhance Krebs cycle synergy.

supplements.mechanism: Converted by NOS enzymes to nitric oxide and citrulline; citrulline recycles to arginine in kidney.

supplements.name: L-Leucine
supplements.summary: The key mTORC1-activating branched-chain amino acid that triggers muscle protein synthesis.
supplements.shortDesc: Primary anabolic trigger for muscle protein synthesis.
supplements.mechanism: Activates mTORC1→S6K1 pathway phosphorylating eIF4E-BP1 to initiate ribosomal 43S complex formation.
sideEffects: Possible insulin resistance at chronic excess

supplements.name: L-Isoleucine
supplements.summary: A branched-chain amino acid supporting glucose uptake into muscle cells and immune function.
supplements.shortDesc: Glucose uptake and muscle energy during exercise.
supplements.mechanism: Stimulates GLUT4 translocation in muscle independently of insulin; activates mTOR to lesser degree than leucine.
sideEffects: Ketogenic interference at very high doses

supplements.name: L-Valine
supplements.summary: The third BCAA that supports muscle metabolism, CNS function, and nitrogen balance.
supplements.shortDesc: Supports muscle energy and cognitive function.
supplements.mechanism: Glucogenic precursor sparing glucose; transported into brain competing with tryptophan reducing serotonin (exercise).

supplements.name: HMB (Beta-Hydroxy Beta-Methylbutyrate)
supplements.summary: A leucine metabolite that reduces muscle protein breakdown and accelerates recovery, particularly in untrained.
supplements.shortDesc: Anti-catabolic leucine metabolite for muscle preservation.

forms.name: Leucine (parent)
forms.notes: 5–10 g leucine produces ~0.5 g HMB endogenously.

supplements.mechanism: Inhibits ubiquitin-proteasome pathway reducing muscle protein catabolism; activates mTOR; may inhibit HMG-CoA reductase.

supplements.name: Taurine
supplements.summary: A conditionally essential sulfur amino acid with diverse roles in cardiovascular health, osmoregulation, bile acid conjugation, neurological function, and exercise performance.
supplements.shortDesc: Multi-role amino acid for heart health, energy drinks, and cellular protection.
supplements.mechanism: Osmoregulates cell volume; conjugates bile acids; activates GABA and glycine receptors; scavenges hypochlorous acid; improves mitochondrial function via calcium regulation.
sideEffects: Very well tolerated; rare GI upset

supplements.name: Glycine
supplements.summary: The simplest amino acid, serving as both an inhibitory neurotransmitter and a structural component of collagen and glutathione, with proven sleep-improving and anti-aging effects.
supplements.shortDesc: Simplest amino acid for sleep quality, collagen synthesis, and longevity.
benefits: Core body temperature reduction at night
supplements.mechanism: Inhibitory neurotransmitter at spinal cord glycine receptors; reduces core body temperature to improve sleep onset; anti-inflammatory via glycine-gated chloride channels; methyl acceptor role.
supplements.bestTime: Before bed (3 g for sleep); with meals for collagen/detox

supplements.name: L-Lysine
supplements.summary: An essential amino acid required for collagen formation, calcium absorption, and herpes simplex virus suppression.
supplements.shortDesc: Collagen formation and herpes suppression.
supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for procollagen hydroxylation; competes with arginine reducing HSV replication; increases intestinal calcium transport.
supplements.bestTime: With meals (or between meals for HSV)
sideEffects: Kidney stone risk in susceptible individuals

supplements.name: L-Proline
supplements.summary: A non-essential amino acid critical for collagen triple-helix structure and cartilage repair.
supplements.shortDesc: Collagen structure and cartilage synthesis.

forms.name: L-Proline powder
forms.notes: Free amino acid; pairs with hydroxyproline.

supplements.mechanism: Hydroxylated to hydroxyproline by prolyl hydroxylase (vitamin C dependent); stabilizes collagen triple-helix.

supplements.name: L-Carnitine (Tartrate)
supplements.summary: Carnitine transports long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation and ATP production.
supplements.shortDesc: Fat-burning cofactor and recovery accelerator.

forms.name: Propionyl-L-Carnitine (GPC)
forms.notes: Best for heart and vascular disease.

supplements.mechanism: Shuttles acyl groups across inner mitochondrial membrane via carnitine-acylcarnitine translocase for β-oxidation.
sideEffects: Fishy body odor at high doses

supplements.name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR)
supplements.summary: The brain-penetrating form of carnitine that supports mitochondrial function, acetylcholine synthesis, and neuroprotection.
supplements.shortDesc: Brain energy and neuroprotection.
supplements.mechanism: Donates acetyl group for ACh synthesis; improves mitochondrial coupling; antioxidant protecting neurons from age-related damage.

supplements.name: L-Tyrosine
supplements.summary: A dopamine and norepinephrine precursor that replenishes catecholamines depleted by stress, sleep deprivation, or cold.
supplements.shortDesc: Dopamine precursor for focus and stress resilience.
supplements.mechanism: Hydroxylated to L-DOPA then dopamine; precursor for NE and E; incorporated into thyroid hormones T3/T4.

supplements.name: GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid)
supplements.summary: The primary inhibitory neurotransmitter; oral GABA has limited CNS penetration but shows stress and sleep benefits.
supplements.shortDesc: Calming neurotransmitter for stress and sleep.

forms.name: Pharma GABA
forms.notes: Fermented GABA; better evidence for oral effect.

supplements.mechanism: Peripheral GABA receptors in gut vagal afferents may relay calming signals centrally; direct parasympathetic activation.

supplements.name: D-Aspartic Acid (DAA)
supplements.summary: An endogenous amino acid in the hypothalamus that stimulates GnRH and LH release, boosting testosterone transiently.
supplements.shortDesc: Transient testosterone boost via LH stimulation.
supplements.mechanism: Stimulates N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA) receptors in hypothalamus increasing GnRH then pituitary LH and testosterone.

supplements.name: Alpha-GPC
supplements.summary: The most bioavailable choline source that crosses the BBB, directly boosting acetylcholine for cognition and performance.
supplements.shortDesc: Best choline supplement for brain and strength.

forms.name: Alpha-GPC 99%
forms.notes: Higher purity; more expensive per gram.

supplements.mechanism: Cleaved to choline in intestinal wall; choline crosses BBB via CHT1 transporter; acetylated to ACh by ChAT.

supplements.name: Citicoline (CDP-Choline)
supplements.summary: CDP-Choline provides both choline and cytidine (converted to uridine), supporting phosphatidylcholine membrane synthesis.
supplements.shortDesc: Dual-action choline for brain and membrane health.

forms.name: Citicoline (Cognizin®)
forms.notes: Proprietary form with most clinical evidence.

supplements.mechanism: Metabolized to choline + cytidine; cytidine converts to uridine in brain; uridine + choline synthesize phosphatidylcholine for synaptic membranes.

supplements.name: Huperzine A
supplements.summary: A potent reversible acetylcholinesterase inhibitor from club moss, used for Alzheimer's and memory enhancement.
supplements.shortDesc: Preserves acetylcholine by inhibiting its breakdown.
supplements.mechanism: Reversibly inhibits acetylcholinesterase with higher selectivity for brain than peripheral AChE.

supplements.name: Noopept (GVS-111)
supplements.summary: A synthetic dipeptide nootropic 1000x more potent than piracetam; increases BDNF, NGF, and Alpha/Beta brain waves.
supplements.shortDesc: Ultra-potent nootropic peptide for memory and BDNF.
supplements.mechanism: Metabolized to cycloprolylglycine activating AMPA receptors; increases BDNF and NGF gene expression.

supplements.name: Lion's Mane (Hericium erinaceus)
supplements.summary: Hericenones and erinacines stimulate NGF synthesis, promoting neurogenesis and slowing cognitive decline.
supplements.shortDesc: NGF-stimulating mushroom for brain growth and repair.

forms.name: Lion's Mane Fruiting Body Extract (30% beta-glucans)
Lion's Mane Mycelium
forms.notes: Lower erinacine content than fruiting body.

forms.name: Lion's Mane Dual Extract
forms.notes: Both water + alcohol extraction captures all actives.

supplements.mechanism: Erinacines (mycelium) and hericenones (fruiting body) cross BBB; stimulate NGF mRNA transcription in hippocampus.
sideEffects: Possible respiratory effects in mushroom allergy

supplements.name: Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum)
supplements.summary: Triterpenes and beta-glucans in reishi modulate immunity, reduce inflammation, and support sleep and longevity.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune-modulating longevity mushroom.
supplements.mechanism: Beta-glucans bind Dectin-1 receptor activating NK cells and macrophages; triterpenes inhibit histamine and leukotriene release.

supplements.name: Turkey Tail (Trametes versicolor)
supplements.summary: A polypore mushroom containing PSK and PSP polysaccharopeptides with robust immune-modulating and anti-cancer adjunct properties, widely studied in oncology.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune-modulating mushroom with anti-cancer adjunct properties.
supplements.mechanism: PSK and PSP polysaccharopeptides activate dendritic cells, NK cells, and T-lymphocytes; modulate TLR2/4 signaling; support beneficial gut bacteria.

supplements.name: Chaga (Inonotus obliquus)
supplements.summary: A parasitic birch fungus rich in betulinic acid derivatives, melanin, and polysaccharides with antioxidant activity.
supplements.shortDesc: Antioxidant and immune-supporting mushroom.

forms.name: Chaga Extract (dual)
forms.notes: Both water and alcohol soluble actives.

supplements.mechanism: Melanin complex and betulinic acid activate macrophage NO production; inotodiol inhibits NF-κB reducing inflammation.
sideEffects: Oxalate accumulation in kidney at high doses

supplements.name: Cordyceps (Sinensis/Militaris)
supplements.summary: Cordycepin and adenosine analogues in cordyceps increase ATP production, VO2 max, and adaptogenic resilience.
supplements.shortDesc: Energy-boosting performance mushroom.
supplements.mechanism: Cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine) mimics adenosine activating AMPK; stimulates adrenocortical cells increasing testosterone and cortisol adaptation.

supplements.name: Maitake (Grifola frondosa)
supplements.summary: D-fraction beta-glucans in maitake potently activate macrophages, NK cells, and T-cells with adjunct anti-tumor activity.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune-activating and blood sugar mushroom.
supplements.mechanism: Beta-1,3/1,6-glucans of D-fraction bind CR3 (Mac-1) receptor on macrophages; stimulate IFN-γ and TNF-α production.

supplements.name: Shiitake (Lentinula edodes)
supplements.summary: Lentinan beta-glucan and eritadenine in shiitake support immune function and cardiovascular health.
supplements.shortDesc: Immune and cholesterol-lowering culinary mushroom.

dosage.male: 5–10 g dried mushroom or 1000–2000 mg extract

forms.name: Dried Shiitake
forms.notes: 2–3 mushrooms per day; excellent culinary source.

supplements.mechanism: Lentinan activates macrophages and NK cells; eritadenine inhibits COMT reducing S-adenosyl-homocysteine increasing methylation.
sideEffects: Rare dermatitis (shiitake dermatitis from raw mushroom)

supplements.name: NR (Nicotinamide Riboside)
supplements.summary: A nucleoside form of vitamin B3 that efficiently raises NAD+ through the NRK1/2 salvage pathway.
supplements.shortDesc: NAD+ booster via NRK salvage pathway.

forms.name: NR + Pterostilbene (Basis)
forms.notes: Combined product with NAD+ and sirtuin activator.

supplements.mechanism: Phosphorylated by NRK1/2 to NMN then NAD+; raises blood NAD+ 40–60% in human RCTs.

supplements.name: Pterostilbene
supplements.summary: A dimethylated resveratrol analog with 4x better bioavailability, superior SIRT1 activation, and longer half-life.
supplements.shortDesc: Superior resveratrol analog for longevity.

forms.name: Pterostilbene (pure)
forms.notes: Better than resveratrol on most metrics.

supplements.mechanism: Dimethyl ether groups at 3,5-position increase lipophilicity and CYP resistance; more potent SIRT1 activation than resveratrol.
sideEffects: Possible increased LDL in some individuals

supplements.name: Fisetin
supplements.summary: A potent senolytic flavonoid that selectively clears senescent cells and activates sirtuin longevity pathways.
supplements.shortDesc: Clears zombie cells; potent anti-aging flavonoid.

supplements.name: Iron (Carbonyl Iron)

supplements.shortDesc: Pure elemental iron with gentler GI profile for anemia.

forms.name: Ferrous Bisglycinate
forms.notes: Chelated form; best tolerated ionic iron.

forms.name: Ferric Pyrophosphate
forms.notes: Used in fortified foods and infant formula.

supplements.mechanism: Absorbed as elemental iron in duodenum; incorporated into hemoglobin and ferritin stores.
supplements.bestTime: Morning on empty stomach with vitamin C
supplements.withFood: Empty stomach for best absorption; food reduces GI effects

supplements.name: Selenium Methyl-Selenocysteine (MSC)
supplements.summary: A naturally occurring selenium compound found in garlic and broccoli with potent anti-cancer properties and superior safety profile over selenomethionine.
supplements.shortDesc: Natural selenium from garlic with anti-cancer and antioxidant properties.

forms.name: Se-Methyl-L-Selenocysteine
forms.notes: Found in selenium-enriched garlic; preferred for cancer prevention.

forms.name: Selenomethionine
forms.notes: Most common supplemental form; accumulates in proteins.

forms.name: Sodium Selenite
forms.notes: Inorganic form; used in IV and clinical settings.

supplements.mechanism: Incorporated into selenoproteins including glutathione peroxidase (GPx) and thioredoxin reductase for redox regulation.

supplements.name: Nascent Iodine
supplements.summary: An atomically distinct form of iodine held in an electromagnetic field, claimed by proponents to be more bioavailable and gentler than Lugol's or potassium iodide.

forms.name: Nascent Iodine
forms.notes: Atomically charged iodine; gentle on thyroid.

forms.name: Lugol's Iodine (5%)

forms.name: Povidone-Iodine

supplements.withFood: Away from food for best absorption

supplements.name: Boron Glycinate
supplements.summary: A chelated form of boron linked to glycine amino acid, supporting testosterone metabolism, estrogen metabolism, bone density, and brain function.
supplements.shortDesc: Chelated boron for hormone balance, bone health, and cognitive function.

forms.name: Boron Fructoborate

supplements.mechanism: Modulates steroid hormone metabolism; enhances vitamin D and magnesium function; inhibits inflammatory enzymes.

supplements.name: Manganese Bisglycinate

supplements.shortDesc: Chelated manganese for bone health and mitochondrial antioxidant defense.
supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for arginase, pyruvate carboxylase, MnSOD; essential for chondroitin sulfate synthesis in cartilage.

supplements.name: Chromium Polynicotinate
supplements.summary: A niacin-bound form of chromium that enhances insulin signaling and glucose uptake, commonly used for blood sugar management and weight control.
supplements.shortDesc: Niacin-bound chromium for blood sugar regulation and insulin sensitivity.

forms.name: Chromium Picolinate
forms.notes: Most studied form; picolinic acid chelate.

supplements.mechanism: Activates chromodulin (LMWCr) to amplify insulin receptor tyrosine kinase activity and glucose transporter (GLUT4) expression.

supplements.name: Molybdenum Glycinate
supplements.summary: A chelated form of the trace mineral molybdenum, a cofactor for enzymes that process sulfur amino acids, uric acid, and detoxify sulfites.
supplements.shortDesc: Trace mineral for sulfur metabolism and sulfite detoxification.

forms.name: Ammonium Molybdate
forms.notes: Inorganic form; used in clinical settings.

supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for sulfite oxidase, xanthine oxidase, aldehyde oxidase enzymes involved in amino acid catabolism and purine metabolism.

supplements.name: Vanadyl Sulfate
supplements.summary: A vanadium compound with insulin-mimetic properties that may improve glucose uptake and is popular among bodybuilders for muscle glycogen storage.
supplements.shortDesc: Trace mineral with insulin-like effects for blood sugar and muscle glycogen.

forms.name: Vanadyl Sulfate
forms.notes: Most common supplemental form; some GI effects.

forms.name: Bis(maltolato)oxovanadium (BMOV)
forms.notes: Organic vanadium complex with improved bioavailability.

supplements.mechanism: Mimics insulin signaling by inhibiting protein tyrosine phosphatases; activates PI3K/Akt pathway for GLUT4 translocation.

supplements.name: Silica (Horsetail Extract)
supplements.summary: Organic silicon dioxide from horsetail plant, essential for collagen synthesis, bone mineralization, and the strength of hair, skin, and nails.

forms.name: Horsetail Extract (Equisetum)

forms.name: Silicon Dioxide (Food Additive)
forms.notes: Used as anti-caking agent; poorly absorbed.

supplements.mechanism: Silicon incorporated into glycosaminoglycan and collagen cross-linking; promotes osteoblast activity and hydroxyapatite formation.

supplements.name: L-Tryptophan
supplements.summary: An essential amino acid and precursor to serotonin, melatonin, and niacin, supporting mood regulation, sleep quality, and appetite control.
supplements.shortDesc: Essential amino acid for serotonin and melatonin production.

forms.name: L-Tryptophan Free Form
forms.notes: Works via kynurenine and serotonin pathways.

forms.name: 5-HTP (5-Hydroxytryptophan)
forms.notes: One step closer to serotonin than tryptophan.

supplements.mechanism: Converted to 5-HTP then serotonin, or to kynurenine (immune/mood regulation) or NAD+ precursors (via niacin pathway).
supplements.withFood: With carbohydrates (improves brain uptake by reducing competing amino acids)

supplements.name: L-Phenylalanine
supplements.summary: An essential amino acid and precursor to tyrosine, dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine, supporting focus, mood, and pain management.
supplements.shortDesc: Dopamine and norepinephrine precursor for focus and mood.

forms.name: L-Phenylalanine
forms.notes: Natural L-form; precursor to tyrosine and catecholamines.

forms.name: D-Phenylalanine

forms.name: DL-Phenylalanine (DLPA)

supplements.mechanism: Hydroxylated to L-tyrosine, then converted to L-DOPA → dopamine → norepinephrine → epinephrine by sequential enzymes.
supplements.withFood: Empty stomach for best CNS uptake

supplements.name: L-Histidine
supplements.summary: A semi-essential amino acid precursor to histamine, important for immune response, stomach acid production, and the antioxidant dipeptide carnosine.
supplements.shortDesc: Amino acid for immune function, gastric acid regulation, and carnosine synthesis.
supplements.mechanism: Decarboxylated to histamine; combined with beta-alanine to form carnosine (muscle buffer, antioxidant); regulates gastric acid.
sideEffects: Excess histamine symptoms at very high doses

supplements.name: L-Methionine
supplements.summary: An essential sulfur-containing amino acid and precursor to SAMe, taurine, cysteine, and glutathione, critical for methylation and liver detoxification.
supplements.shortDesc: Sulfur amino acid for methylation, liver detox, and antioxidant synthesis.
supplements.mechanism: Converted to SAMe (universal methyl donor); metabolized via transsulfuration to cysteine → glutathione; supports one-carbon metabolism.

supplements.name: Essential Amino Acids (EAA) Blend
supplements.summary: A complete blend of all 9 essential amino acids providing the full spectrum required for muscle protein synthesis, superior to BCAAs alone.
supplements.shortDesc: Complete essential amino acid blend for maximal muscle protein synthesis.

forms.name: EAA Powder Blend
forms.notes: All 9 EAAs including BCAAs; superior to BCAA alone.

supplements.mechanism: Provides all essential amino acids for ribosomal protein synthesis; leucine component activates mTORC1; remaining EAAs provide substrates.
sideEffects: Mild GI discomfort at high doses

references.title: EAAs vs BCAAs for muscle synthesis

supplements.name: Beta-Hydroxybutyrate (BHB)
supplements.summary: An exogenous ketone body that elevates blood ketone levels without strict fasting, providing an efficient brain and muscle fuel and supporting fat adaptation.
supplements.shortDesc: Exogenous ketone for brain fuel, fat loss, and ketogenic performance.

forms.name: BHB Esters (R-1,3-Butanediol BHB)

forms.name: C8 MCT Oil (Caprylic Acid)

supplements.bestTime: Morning fasted, pre-workout, or during intermittent fasting
supplements.withFood: Can take fasted for best results

supplements.name: Epicatechin

supplements.shortDesc: Dark chocolate flavanol that inhibits myostatin for muscle growth.

forms.name: Epicatechin Powder/Capsule

supplements.mechanism: Inhibits myostatin and activin receptor IIB; activates follistatin; enhances NOS3 for NO production; improves mitochondrial biogenesis.
sideEffects: Possible stimulant sensitivity from combined caffeine in cacao

supplements.name: 5-Alpha-Hydroxy-Laxogenin

supplements.shortDesc: Natural plant steroid analogue for muscle protein synthesis.

supplements.name: Peak ATP (Adenosine 5-Triphosphate)
supplements.summary: An oral form of adenosine triphosphate (ATP) that has been shown to improve muscular excitability, blood flow, and exercise performance through extracellular signaling.
supplements.shortDesc: Oral ATP for improved exercise capacity, blood flow, and muscle excitability.

supplements.name: Nitrosigine (Arginine Silicate Inositol)

supplements.name: S7 Nitric Oxide Blend

supplements.shortDesc: Plant-based blend boosting endogenous nitric oxide production by 230%.

forms.name: S7 Blend (trademarked)

supplements.name: Beetroot Extract (Nitrate)

supplements.name: Sodium Bicarbonate (Sports Use)

supplements.name: Tart Cherry Extract

supplements.mechanism: Anthocyanins inhibit COX-1/COX-2 enzymes; melatonin content improves sleep; proanthocyanidins reduce urate crystals in gout.

supplements.name: Pomegranate Extract (Punicalagins)

supplements.name: Black Pepper Extract (Piperine/BioPerine)

supplements.name: Rehmannia (Shu Di Huang / Steamed)

supplements.name: Black Seed Oil (Nigella sativa / Thymoquinone)

supplements.name: Pine Pollen Extract

supplements.name: Sulforaphane (Broccoli Sprout Extract)

supplements.withFood: With mustard seed or food containing myrosinase

supplements.name: Luteolin

supplements.name: Myricetin

supplements.name: Kaempferol

supplements.name: Pycnogenol (French Maritime Pine Bark Extract)

supplements.name: Amla (Indian Gooseberry / Emblica officinalis)

supplements.name: Ashwagandha Shoden (35% Withanolide Glycosides)

supplements.name: Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma longifolia / Longjack)

supplements.name: 5-HTP (5-Hydroxytryptophan)

supplements.name: SAMe (S-Adenosyl Methionine)
