forms.name: Ipriflavone Capsules
forms.notes: Standard supplemental form; take with calcium and D3.

supplements.mechanism: Inhibits osteoclast differentiation and activity via calcitonin receptor upregulation; enhances calcium incorporation into bone; mild estrogenic activity.
supplements.bestTime: With meals, three times per day
sideEffects: Lymphocyte reduction with long-term use (lymphopenia risk)

supplements.name: DHEA (Dehydroepiandrosterone)
supplements.summary: A major adrenal hormone that declines with age, serving as a precursor to both testosterone and estrogen, used for anti-aging, adrenal support, and hormonal balance.
supplements.shortDesc: Adrenal precursor hormone for anti-aging, testosterone, and adrenal support.

forms.name: DHEA Standard Capsule
forms.notes: Most common form; 7-Keto DHEA is non-hormonal metabolite.

forms.name: 7-Keto DHEA
forms.notes: Does not convert to sex hormones; used for metabolism.

forms.name: Micronized DHEA
forms.notes: Smaller particle size for improved bioavailability.

supplements.mechanism: Converted to androstenedione → testosterone/estrogen in peripheral tissues; activates peroxisome proliferator-activated receptors (PPARs); inhibits G6PDH (anti-tumor).

supplements.name: Melatonin Extended-Release
supplements.summary: A timed-release melatonin formulation that maintains elevated blood levels throughout the night, better supporting sleep architecture and circadian rhythm maintenance.
supplements.shortDesc: Time-release melatonin for all-night sleep support and circadian rhythm.
supplements.mechanism: MT1 and MT2 receptor agonist on SCN circadian clock; inhibits adenylyl cyclase; powerful mitochondrial antioxidant; regulates immune function; lowers core body temperature.
supplements.withFood: On empty stomach or light snack
sideEffects: Hormonal effects with long-term high dose

supplements.name: L-Carnitine Tartrate
supplements.summary: A stable salt form of L-carnitine that transports long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for oxidation, making it popular for fat burning and exercise recovery.
supplements.shortDesc: Fast-absorbing carnitine salt for fat metabolism and exercise recovery.

forms.name: L-Carnitine L-Tartrate (LCLT)
forms.notes: Fastest absorbed carnitine form; ideal pre/post workout.

forms.name: L-Carnitine Fumarate
forms.notes: Fumarate enters Krebs cycle alongside carnitine.

forms.name: Propionyl-L-Carnitine (PLC)
forms.notes: Best form for heart and peripheral vascular disease.

supplements.mechanism: Shuttles long-chain acyl groups across inner mitochondrial membrane (via CPT1/CPT2); required for beta-oxidation; ALCAR provides acetyl groups for acetylcholine.
supplements.withFood: With carbohydrate meal for best muscle uptake
sideEffects: Potential atherosclerosis risk via TMAO (conflicting data)

supplements.name: CDP-Choline (Citicoline / Cognizin)
supplements.summary: A highly bioavailable choline source that also provides cytidine (converted to uridine in the brain), supporting phosphatidylcholine synthesis, dopamine function, and neuroprotection.
supplements.shortDesc: Dual nootropic providing choline and uridine for brain membrane repair and dopamine.

forms.name: CDP-Choline Generic
forms.bestFor: Same as Cognizin at lower cost

supplements.mechanism: Hydrolyzed to choline and cytidine → cytidine → uridine in brain; choline → acetylcholine + phosphatidylcholine; uridine activates P2Y receptors and supports membrane formation.

supplements.name: Uridine Monophosphate
supplements.summary: A pyrimidine nucleotide precursor that crosses the blood-brain barrier to support RNA synthesis, phosphatidylcholine production, dopamine receptor density, and synaptic plasticity.
supplements.shortDesc: Nucleotide for brain membrane repair, dopamine support, and synaptic growth.

forms.name: Triacetyluridine (TAU)
forms.notes: Acetylated form with 3x higher brain levels.

supplements.mechanism: Converts to UTP → activates P2Y purinergic receptors → promotes neurite outgrowth (with DHA); precursor for phosphatidylcholine via Kennedy pathway; increases striatal dopamine release.

supplements.name: PEA (Phenylethylamine HCl)
supplements.summary: A trace amine and endogenous neuromodulator that triggers dopamine and norepinephrine release, producing rapid mood elevation, focus, and feelings of attraction or excitement.
supplements.shortDesc: Trace amine for rapid mood elevation, dopamine release, and focus.

forms.name: PEA HCl
forms.bestFor: Acute mood boost, focus, appetite suppression
forms.notes: Broken down rapidly by MAO-B; effects last 10–20 min.

forms.name: PEA + MAO-B Inhibitor (Hordenine)
forms.notes: Hordenine slows MAO-B breakdown of PEA.

supplements.mechanism: Activates trace amine-associated receptor 1 (TAAR1); triggers vesicular dopamine and norepinephrine release; inhibits dopamine reuptake; activates AMPK.
supplements.bestTime: Morning on empty stomach for mood; pre-workout for focus

supplements.name: N-Acetyl L-Tyrosine (NALT)
supplements.summary: An acetylated form of the amino acid tyrosine that supports dopamine and norepinephrine synthesis under stress, improving cognitive performance during high-demand situations.
supplements.shortDesc: Acetylated tyrosine for catecholamine support under stress and cognitive demand.

forms.name: N-Acetyl L-Tyrosine (NALT)
forms.bestFor: Cognitive performance under stress, military use

forms.name: L-Tyrosine Free Form
forms.notes: Slightly higher tyrosine content per mg.

supplements.mechanism: Tyrosine → L-DOPA → dopamine → norepinephrine → epinephrine via enzyme cascade; thyroid peroxidase uses tyrosine for T3/T4 synthesis; precursor to melanin.
supplements.bestTime: Before mentally demanding tasks or exercise; morning
supplements.withFood: Empty stomach for best CNS uptake

references.title: Tyrosine and cognitive performance under stress

supplements.name: Phosphatidylserine (PS)
supplements.summary: A phospholipid found in every cell but concentrated in neural tissue, essential for neurotransmitter release, cortisol reduction, and cognitive performance under stress.
supplements.shortDesc: Brain phospholipid for cortisol reduction, memory, and cognitive performance.

forms.name: Sunflower Lecithin PS
forms.notes: Non-soy; preferred by those avoiding soy.

supplements.mechanism: Component of inner plasma membrane leaflet; essential for neurotransmitter exocytosis; activates Na+/K+-ATPase; modulates HPA axis by lowering ACTH/cortisol; supports myelination.
supplements.bestTime: With meals; pre-exercise to reduce cortisol

supplements.name: Lion's Mane Dual Extract (NGF Support)
supplements.summary: A comprehensive hot water and alcohol dual extract capturing both beta-glucan polysaccharides and the lipid-soluble hericenones and erinacines for full NGF and immune support.
supplements.shortDesc: Full-spectrum lion's mane with immune and NGF benefits in one extract.

forms.name: Dual Extract (1:1 hot water + alcohol)
forms.notes: Most comprehensive; optimal balance of both fractions.

supplements.mechanism: Beta-glucans activate TLR2 immune receptors; hericenones/erinacines induce NGF and BDNF synthesis in astrocytes; support gut-brain axis via enteric nervous system.

supplements.name: Saffron Extract (Affron / Safr'Inside)
supplements.summary: A standardized saffron extract with clinical evidence for depression, anxiety, PMS, eye health, and memory, acting through serotonin reuptake inhibition and BDNF modulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Standardized saffron for depression, anxiety, PMS, and eye protection.
supplements.mechanism: Safranal inhibits serotonin and dopamine reuptake; crocin increases BDNF and protects retinal photoreceptors; modulates GABA-A; has anti-inflammatory effects via NF-kB inhibition.

supplements.name: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata)
supplements.summary: A traditional anxiety and sleep herb containing chrysin and vitexin that modulates GABA-A receptors, comparable to oxazepam for generalized anxiety.
supplements.shortDesc: GABA-A modulating herb for anxiety, insomnia, and stress equivalent to oxazepam.
supplements.mechanism: Chrysin binds GABA-A benzodiazepine site; harmane alkaloids inhibit MAO-A (mild); vitexin reduces cortisol; flavonoids modulate adenosine receptors for sedation.
supplements.bestTime: 30–60 min before bed or stressful events

supplements.name: Melatonin Low-Dose (0.3–0.5 mg)
supplements.summary: A physiologically appropriate micro-dose of melatonin that mimics natural nighttime secretion levels, improving sleep quality with fewer side effects than conventional high doses.
supplements.shortDesc: Physiological micro-dose melatonin for clean sleep onset without grogginess.

forms.name: 0.3–0.5 mg melatonin tablet
forms.notes: Closest to physiological pineal output (~0.1–0.3 mg).

supplements.mechanism: MT1 receptor activation signals 'biological night' to SCN; lowers core body temperature; initiates sleep pressure; does NOT increase sleep pressure via adenosine.
supplements.bestTime: 30 min before target sleep time (consistent timing crucial)

supplements.name: L-Ornithine
supplements.summary: A non-essential amino acid involved in the urea cycle that promotes ammonia clearance and has notable sleep-improving and growth hormone-releasing properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Urea cycle amino acid for ammonia clearance, sleep, and GH release.

forms.name: L-Ornithine HCl
forms.notes: Standard supplemental form; effective at 400 mg.

forms.name: Ornithine Alpha-Ketoglutarate (OKG)
forms.notes: Provides both ornithine and AKG for recovery.

supplements.mechanism: Orinthine transcarbamylase converts ornithine to citrulline in urea cycle removing toxic ammonia; stimulates pituitary GH release; GABA-like calming mechanism for sleep.
supplements.withFood: Empty stomach for GH and sleep effects

supplements.name: Inulin (Prebiotic Fiber)
supplements.summary: A soluble dietary fiber and prebiotic that selectively feeds Bifidobacteria and Lactobacilli in the colon, improving gut microbiome diversity and metabolic health.
supplements.shortDesc: Prebiotic fiber for Bifidobacteria growth, gut health, and metabolic benefits.

forms.name: Inulin Powder (from chicory)
forms.notes: Most common source; mix in water or food.

forms.name: Agave Inulin
forms.notes: Slower fermentation; less gas than chicory.

supplements.mechanism: Passes undigested to colon; fermented by Bifidobacteria to SCFAs (butyrate, propionate, acetate); lowers colonic pH; stimulates mucin production; modulates incretin release.
supplements.withFood: With food (start gradually to reduce gas)

supplements.name: Resistant Starch (RS2/RS3)
supplements.summary: A type of dietary starch that resists digestion in the small intestine and acts as a prebiotic in the colon, specifically promoting butyrate-producing bacteria and improving metabolic health.
supplements.shortDesc: Prebiotic starch for butyrate production, insulin sensitivity, and gut health.

forms.name: Raw Potato Starch (RS2)
forms.notes: Most concentrated RS source; cook but cool before eating.

supplements.mechanism: Escapes small intestinal digestion → fermented by Firmicutes and Bacteroidetes in colon → butyrate → colon cell fuel → reduces insulin resistance via adipose tissue signaling.
sideEffects: Gas and bloating initially (start with 5 g/day)

supplements.name: Sodium Butyrate / Tributyrin
supplements.summary: A short-chain fatty acid that serves as primary fuel for colonocytes, supporting gut barrier integrity, reducing intestinal inflammation, and providing systemic epigenetic effects.
supplements.shortDesc: Colonocyte fuel for gut barrier repair, inflammation reduction, and HDAC inhibition.

forms.name: Tributyrin (triglyceride form)
forms.notes: Butyrate esterified to glycerol; releases in colon.

forms.name: Sodium Butyrate (enteric-coated)
forms.notes: Common salt form; enteric coating reduces upper GI release.

supplements.mechanism: Oxidized by colonocytes for ATP (preferred fuel); inhibits HDAC class I/II (epigenetic regulation); reduces NF-kB; strengthens tight junctions via claudin and occludin upregulation.

supplements.name: Ceylon Cinnamon (True Cinnamon)
supplements.summary: The true cinnamon species (C. verum) with lower coumarin content than cassia, supporting blood sugar regulation, anti-inflammatory activity, and antimicrobial effects.
supplements.shortDesc: True cinnamon for blood sugar regulation with lower toxicity than cassia.

dosage.upperLimit: 6 g/day (cassia limit lower due to coumarin)

forms.name: Ceylon Cinnamon (C. verum) Powder
forms.notes: Very low coumarin; safe for daily use.

forms.name: Cassia Cinnamon (C. cassia)
forms.bestFor: Blood sugar (not for daily high doses)
forms.notes: Common grocery cinnamon; high coumarin — limit to 1g/day.

supplements.mechanism: Polyphenols (type-A proanthocyanidins) activate insulin receptor signaling; GLUT4 translocation; inhibit alpha-glucosidase; anti-biofilm activity; NF-kB inhibition.
sideEffects: Liver damage at very high doses from cassia coumarin

supplements.name: Trans-Resveratrol (Polygonum cuspidatum)
supplements.summary: The active stilbene form of resveratrol from Japanese knotweed root, activating SIRT1 longevity pathway, reducing inflammation, and supporting cardiovascular and metabolic health.
supplements.shortDesc: Trans-resveratrol stilbene for SIRT1 activation, longevity, and cardiovascular health.

forms.name: Trans-Resveratrol (Polygonum cuspidatum)
forms.notes: Most common source; very low oral bioavailability.

forms.name: Micronized Trans-Resveratrol
forms.notes: Smaller particle size for better mucosal absorption.

forms.name: Resveratrol Piceid (trans-resveratrol-3-O-glucoside)
forms.notes: Glucoside form with different absorption kinetics.

supplements.mechanism: Allosterically activates SIRT1 deacetylase → modulates PGC-1α, FOXO, p53; inhibits COX-1/2; activates AMPK; inhibits NF-kB; modulates estrogen receptors.
supplements.bestTime: With fatty meal for best absorption
supplements.withFood: Required — fat-soluble and increases bioavailability significantly

supplements.name: Horse Chestnut Extract (Aescin)
supplements.summary: A standardized seed extract containing the triterpene glycoside aescin, with robust clinical evidence for chronic venous insufficiency, varicose veins, and edema.
supplements.shortDesc: Aescin-containing extract for varicose veins, chronic venous insufficiency, and edema.

forms.name: Horse Chestnut Seed Extract (20% aescin)
forms.notes: Standardized to aescin content; most studied.

supplements.mechanism: Aescin seals capillary permeability by inhibiting lysosomal enzymes that degrade capillary proteoglycans; enhances venous tone; inhibits prostaglandin synthesis.

supplements.name: Grape Seed Extract (OPC / Proanthocyanidins)
supplements.summary: A rich source of oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) with potent antioxidant, vascular, and anti-inflammatory properties, used for cardiovascular health and skin protection.
supplements.shortDesc: OPC-rich grape seed extract for cardiovascular protection and antioxidant defense.

forms.name: MegaNatural-BP (blood pressure variant)
forms.notes: Patented extract with BP-specific RCT data.

supplements.mechanism: OPCs inhibit NADPH oxidase; collagen-binding inhibits MMP enzymes; reduces LDL oxidation; inhibits ACE for blood pressure; modulates NO production; crosses BBB.

supplements.name: MCT Oil C8 (Caprylic Acid)
supplements.summary: A concentrated medium-chain triglyceride from coconut oil — specifically C8 caprylic acid — that is rapidly converted to ketones in the liver for brain and muscle fuel.
supplements.shortDesc: Pure C8 MCT for fastest ketone production, brain fuel, and fat adaptation.

forms.name: C8 MCT Oil (100% caprylic acid)
forms.notes: Most ketogenic MCT fraction; 3x more than C10.

forms.name: C8/C10 MCT Oil Blend
forms.notes: Most common commercial MCT oil blend.

forms.name: MCT Powder (spray-dried)
forms.notes: Lower ketogenic effect due to carbohydrate carriers.

supplements.mechanism: C8 absorbed directly into portal vein → liver → beta-oxidized to acetyl-CoA → ketone bodies (BHB/acetoacetate) regardless of insulin status; bypasses carnitine transport.
supplements.withFood: Start with food to reduce GI effects; can take fasted once adapted
sideEffects: GI distress, diarrhea if dose increased too fast

supplements.name: Black Currant Seed Oil
supplements.summary: A seed oil containing both GLA and SDA (stearidonic acid), offering dual omega-3 and omega-6 benefits with anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Dual GLA and SDA oil for inflammation, immune modulation, and skin health.
supplements.mechanism: GLA pathway → PGE1; SDA converts more efficiently to EPA than ALA; anthocyanins in whole berry provide additional antioxidant protection.

references.title: Black currant seed oil GLA and SDA

supplements.name: Oil of Oregano (Carvacrol/Thymol)
supplements.summary: A potent essential oil extract from Origanum vulgare containing carvacrol and thymol with broad-spectrum antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-parasitic properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial oil for gut infections, candida, and immune support.

forms.name: Wild Oregano Oil (P73 blend)
forms.notes: Wild Mediterranean oregano; P73 is patented blend.

supplements.mechanism: Carvacrol disrupts bacterial cell membranes; inhibits biofilm formation; penetrates fungal cell walls; thymol acts as phenolic antioxidant; inhibits quorum sensing.
sideEffects: GI burning if taken without food
Kills beneficial bacteria with prolonged use
Allergic reactions in carrot/celery family sensitivity

supplements.name: Elderberry Extract (Sambucus nigra)
supplements.summary: A dark berry extract rich in anthocyanins that inhibit influenza viral entry, reduce cytokine-mediated inflammation, and shorten cold and flu duration by 2–4 days.
supplements.shortDesc: Anthocyanin-rich berry for flu prevention, immune activation, and cold reduction.

forms.name: Elderberry Extract (Sambucol standardized)
forms.notes: Standardized for anthocyanins; most clinically studied.

supplements.mechanism: Anthocyanins bind and block hemagglutinin on influenza virions; stimulate cytokine production (TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6 — caution in cytokine storms); flavonoids inhibit neuraminidase.
supplements.bestTime: At first sign of illness or daily preventive in winter
sideEffects: Possible cytokine storm amplification in severe illness — theoretical concern

supplements.name: Echinacea Purpurea
supplements.summary: The most studied echinacea species, with alkylamides and polysaccharides that stimulate innate immunity, reducing cold incidence and duration through macrophage and NK cell activation.
supplements.shortDesc: Classic immune herb for cold prevention and duration via innate immune activation.

forms.name: Echinacea Pallida (root)
forms.notes: Different alkaloid profile; used in Europe.

supplements.mechanism: Alkylamides bind CB2 receptors modulating immune response; polysaccharides activate macrophages; inhibit hyaluronidase (limits pathogen spread); induces interferon production.
supplements.bestTime: At first sign of cold; 3 divided doses
sideEffects: Potential immunosuppression with very long-term continuous use

supplements.name: Astragalus (Astragalus membranaceus)
supplements.summary: A foundational TCM adaptogen containing astragalosides — particularly cycloastragenol — that activate telomerase, support immune function, and have anti-aging properties.
supplements.shortDesc: TCM adaptogen with telomerase-activating astragalosides for longevity and immunity.
supplements.mechanism: Astragalosides activate telomerase reverse transcriptase (TERT); polysaccharides activate NK cells and macrophages; saponins modulate TLR signaling; hepatoprotective via antioxidant enzymes.
sideEffects: Possible immune over-activation in autoimmune conditions

supplements.name: Larch Arabinogalactan
supplements.summary: A polysaccharide fiber from larch tree bark with prebiotic activity that selectively increases Bifidobacteria and Lactobacilli, enhances NK cell activity, and supports mucosal immunity.
supplements.shortDesc: Larch tree prebiotic fiber for gut microbiome and innate immune enhancement.
supplements.mechanism: Fermented by Bifidobacteria → SCFAs; beta-(1→3)-galactan backbone activates TLR2 on dendritic cells → NK cell activation; increases secretory IgA.
sideEffects: GI cramping if dose increased too fast

supplements.name: Beta-Glucan (Oat / Yeast)
supplements.summary: A soluble fiber from oats and yeast cell walls with powerful immune-modulating, cholesterol-lowering, and blood sugar-stabilizing effects via beta-(1→3),(1→4)-glucan structure.
supplements.shortDesc: Soluble fiber for cholesterol, blood sugar, and innate immune activation.

forms.name: Oat Beta-Glucan (certified)
forms.notes: FDA-approved for heart health claim at 3g/day.

supplements.mechanism: Soluble fiber forms gel in gut reducing bile acid reabsorption → lower LDL; yeast beta-1,3-glucan binds Dectin-1 on macrophages → NK cell and T cell activation.
sideEffects: GI upset if increased too quickly

supplements.name: Milk Thistle (Silybin / Silymarin)
supplements.summary: A hepatoprotective herb whose active flavonolignan silymarin (particularly silybin) protects liver cells from toxins, promotes regeneration, and has anti-fibrotic properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Gold-standard liver-protective herb for detox, hepatitis, and cirrhosis support.

forms.name: Milk Thistle Extract (70–80% silymarin)
forms.notes: Most common form; poor water solubility limits absorption.

supplements.mechanism: Silymarin inhibits hepatic uptake of amatoxins and other toxins; activates Nrf2 → antioxidant enzymes; inhibits TGF-β1 for anti-fibrosis; stabilizes hepatocyte membranes.

supplements.name: Artichoke Leaf Extract (Cynara scolymus)
supplements.summary: A liver and digestive herb containing cynarin and chlorogenic acid that stimulates bile flow, lowers cholesterol, protects the liver, and reduces IBS symptoms.
supplements.shortDesc: Cynarin-rich herb for bile stimulation, cholesterol reduction, and liver support.
supplements.mechanism: Cynarin stimulates hepatic bile production and gallbladder contraction; chlorogenic acid inhibits HMG-CoA reductase; luteolin inhibits NF-kB; promotes intestinal motility.

supplements.name: Moringa (Moringa oleifera)
supplements.summary: A nutrient-dense 'superfood' tree leaf containing exceptional concentrations of vitamins, minerals, amino acids, and isothiocyanates with anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and neuroprotective effects.
supplements.shortDesc: Nutrient-dense superfood leaf for blood sugar, inflammation, and overall nutrition.
supplements.mechanism: Isothiocyanates (moringin) activate Nrf2; chlorogenic acid reduces glucose absorption; zeatin cytokinin has anti-aging properties; high bioavailable iron and calcium content.

supplements.name: Spirulina (Arthrospira platensis)
supplements.summary: A blue-green microalgae with 60–70% complete protein, phycocyanin antioxidant, and extensive benefits for immune function, lipids, blood sugar, and exercise performance.
supplements.shortDesc: High-protein algae with phycocyanin for immune, metabolic, and athletic benefits.
supplements.mechanism: Phycocyanin inhibits 5-LOX and COX-2; c-phycocyanin activates NK cells; beta-carotene → Vitamin A; chlorophyll chelates heavy metals; complete amino acid profile stimulates mTOR.
supplements.bestTime: Morning in smoothie or before exercise

supplements.name: Chlorella (Chlorella vulgaris)
supplements.summary: A single-celled green freshwater algae with exceptional chlorophyll content, a unique growth factor (CGF), and proven heavy metal detoxification properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Chlorophyll-rich algae for heavy metal detox, immune support, and gut health.

forms.name: Broken Cell Wall Chlorella
forms.notes: Cell wall disruption required for nutrient access.

forms.name: Whole Chlorella Powder
forms.notes: Cell walls intact; lower nutrient bioavailability.

supplements.mechanism: Chlorophyll binds dioxins, heavy metals, and heterocyclic amines for fecal elimination; CGF stimulates beneficial bacteria and immune cells; sporopollenin binds cadmium and mercury.
supplements.bestTime: With meals or before a meal

supplements.name: Celery Seed Extract (3nB)
supplements.summary: A concentrated extract containing 3-n-butylphthalide (3nB) with uric acid lowering, anti-hypertensive, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.
supplements.shortDesc: 3nB-containing extract for gout, blood pressure, and uric acid reduction.
supplements.mechanism: 3nB inhibits xanthine oxidase (reduces uric acid); inhibits prostaglandin biosynthesis; modulates calcium channels in smooth muscle for vasodilation; mild diuresis.

references.title: Celery seed extract and uric acid

supplements.name: Bromelain
supplements.summary: A proteolytic enzyme complex from pineapple stems with anti-inflammatory, anti-edema, and immune-modulating effects used for surgical recovery, sinusitis, and digestive health.
supplements.shortDesc: Pineapple protease enzyme for inflammation, swelling, and post-surgical recovery.
supplements.mechanism: Cysteine protease cleaves fibrin and bradykinin → reduces edema; modulates PGE2 and thromboxane; stimulates regulatory T cells; degrades food proteins in digestion.
supplements.bestTime: Between meals for systemic anti-inflammatory; with meals for digestion
supplements.withFood: Empty stomach for inflammation; with food for digestion

supplements.name: Hawthorn Berry (Crataegus oxyacantha)
supplements.summary: A European medicinal herb with extensive history and clinical evidence for heart failure support, blood pressure reduction, and cardiac output improvement via oligomeric proanthocyanidins.
supplements.shortDesc: OPC-rich herb for heart failure support, blood pressure, and cardiac output.

forms.name: Hawthorn Berry Extract (WS 1442)
forms.bestFor: Heart failure, blood pressure, cardiac output
forms.notes: WS 1442 is the most studied standardized extract.

forms.name: Hawthorn Leaf and Flower Extract
forms.notes: Higher OPC content in leaves vs berries.

supplements.mechanism: OPCs inhibit ACE and phosphodiesterase; increase coronary blood flow; protect endothelium via Nrf2; strengthen cardiac contractility; anti-arrhythmic via potassium channel effects.

supplements.name: Butterbur (Petasites hybridus)
supplements.summary: A European herb with clinical evidence comparable to prescription antihistamines for allergic rhinitis and to beta-blockers for migraine prevention, via petasin leukotriene inhibition.
supplements.shortDesc: Petasin-rich herb for migraine prevention and seasonal allergy relief.

forms.name: Butterbur PA-Free Extract (Petadolex)
forms.notes: MUST be pyrrolizidine alkaloid (PA)-free; hepatotoxic otherwise.

supplements.mechanism: Petasin and isopetasin inhibit 5-LOX and COX-2 → block leukotriene and prostaglandin synthesis; anti-spasmodic via calcium channel blocking; reduce CGRP in migraine.
sideEffects: SERIOUS: hepatotoxicity if NOT PA-free extract

supplements.name: R-Alpha-Lipoic Acid (R-ALA)
supplements.summary: The biologically active R-isomer of alpha-lipoic acid, a universal antioxidant recycling vitamin C and E, regenerating glutathione, and supporting glucose metabolism and neuropathy.
supplements.shortDesc: Active R-isomer ALA for antioxidant recycling, blood sugar, and neuropathy.

forms.name: R-Alpha-Lipoic Acid (R-ALA)
forms.notes: R-isomer only; twice as potent as racemic ALA.

forms.name: Racemic ALA (R+S mixture)
forms.notes: Standard ALA; S-isomer may inhibit R-isomer.

supplements.mechanism: Cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase and alpha-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase complexes; regenerates glutathione, vitamins C and E; chelates heavy metals; activates GLUT4; inhibits AGE formation.
supplements.bestTime: 30 min before meals on empty stomach
sideEffects: Biotin depletion with high-dose long-term use
Thyroid effects at very high doses

supplements.name: Glutathione (Liposomal / S-Acetyl)
supplements.summary: The body's master antioxidant, a tripeptide of glutamate, cysteine, and glycine, supplemented in reduced or liposomal form for immune defense, liver detoxification, and anti-aging.
supplements.shortDesc: Master antioxidant in bioavailable liposomal or S-acetyl form for detox and immunity.

forms.name: Liposomal Glutathione
forms.notes: Phospholipid vesicles protect from GI degradation.

forms.name: S-Acetyl Glutathione
forms.notes: Acetyl group prevents oxidation during digestion.

forms.name: Reduced Glutathione (GSH free form)
forms.notes: Degraded in GI tract; use precursors instead.

supplements.mechanism: GSH + toxic compounds → glutathione conjugates → fecal/urinary elimination; reduces glutathione disulfide (GSSG) back to GSH via glutathione reductase; directly neutralizes H2O2 and lipid peroxides.
sideEffects: Possible zinc depletion with long-term high dose

supplements.name: Caffeine + L-Theanine Stack
supplements.summary: The most evidence-backed nootropic combination, pairing caffeine's stimulant effects with L-theanine's anxiety-reducing properties for focused alertness without jitteriness.
supplements.shortDesc: Gold-standard nootropic stack for focused alertness without caffeine jitters.
supplements.mechanism: Caffeine antagonizes adenosine A1/A2A receptors → arousal; L-theanine increases alpha brainwave activity → relaxed focus; theanine modulates caffeine-induced noradrenaline and dopamine release.
supplements.bestTime: Morning or early afternoon; avoid after 2pm

supplements.name: Maitake Mushroom (Grifola frondosa)
supplements.summary: A culinary and medicinal mushroom with the D-fraction beta-glucan (PET) complex demonstrating potent immune activation, anti-cancer, blood sugar regulation, and blood pressure support.
supplements.shortDesc: D-fraction mushroom for immune activation, blood sugar, and anti-cancer support.

forms.name: Maitake Powder (whole mushroom)
forms.notes: Whole fruit body; lower D-fraction concentration.

supplements.mechanism: D-fraction beta-(1→3)(1→6)-glucan binds Dectin-1 on macrophages and NK cells → cytokine cascade; alpha-glucan content activates insulin signaling; eritadenine reduces cholesterol.

supplements.name: Melatonin High-Dose (Cancer Adjunct)
supplements.summary: High-dose melatonin (10–40 mg) used as an oncology adjunct for anti-tumor effects, radiosensitization, and reducing chemotherapy toxicity, distinct from sleep dose.
supplements.shortDesc: High-dose melatonin as cancer adjunct and antioxidant at pharmacological levels.

dosage.beginner: Not for general use — consult oncologist
dosage.upperLimit: No established UL for therapeutic use

forms.name: Melatonin High-Dose Capsule (10–20 mg)
forms.notes: Pharmacological dose; different from sleep use.

supplements.mechanism: At high doses: anti-proliferative via MT1 receptor; anti-angiogenic (reduces VEGF); increases NK cell activity; direct mitochondrial antioxidant; reduces cisplatin nephrotoxicity.

supplements.name: Potassium Iodide
supplements.summary: Pharmaceutical-grade iodine supplement used for thyroid protection, radiation emergencies, and correcting iodine deficiency.
supplements.shortDesc: Pharmaceutical iodine for thyroid protection and deficiency correction.
supplements.mechanism: Iodide incorporated into thyroglobulin by TPO for T3/T4 synthesis; blocks radioactive iodine uptake at high doses.

supplements.name: Calcium Citrate Malate
supplements.summary: A highly bioavailable calcium form absorbed efficiently even without stomach acid, ideal for postmenopausal women and those on acid-suppressing medications.
supplements.shortDesc: Most bioavailable calcium form ideal for low stomach acid or PPI users.
supplements.mechanism: Calcium ions released in intestine via ionization; citrate-malate complex enhances paracellular absorption; malate enters Krebs cycle.
sideEffects: Kidney stone risk with very high intake

supplements.name: D-Mannose

sideEffects: Male infertility concerns at very high prolonged doses

supplements.name: Vitamin D3 5000 IU (Cholecalciferol)
supplements.summary: Cholecalciferol at 5000 IU provides optimal therapeutic dosing for correcting widespread vitamin D deficiency, supporting immune function, bone density, cardiovascular health, and 200+ gene expressions.
supplements.shortDesc: Therapeutic vitamin D3 dose for deficiency correction and immune/bone optimization.

forms.name: Cholecalciferol D3 Softgel (oil-suspended)
forms.notes: Oil-suspended (olive or MCT) for best absorption.

forms.name: Cholecalciferol D3 Tablet
forms.notes: Dry form; lower absorption without fat.

forms.name: Lanolin-Derived D3
forms.notes: Most D3 derived from lanolin (sheep wool).

supplements.mechanism: Cholecalciferol → 25(OH)D (liver) → 1,25(OH)₂D3 (kidney) active form; binds VDR nuclear receptor → gene expression in 200+ tissues; modulates T-cell differentiation (Treg vs Th17); calcium/phosphorus absorption.

supplements.name: Lemon Balm Extract (Melissa officinalis)
supplements.summary: A mint-family herb with rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that reduce anxiety, improve sleep quality, and enhance cognitive performance via GABA transaminase inhibition and acetylcholinesterase inhibition.
supplements.shortDesc: Calming herb for anxiety, sleep, and cognition via GABA and acetylcholine pathways.

forms.name: Lemon Balm Tea
forms.notes: Traditional use; lower potency than extract.

supplements.mechanism: Rosmarinic acid inhibits GABA transaminase (increases GABA availability); inhibits AChE (increases acetylcholine); flavonoids modulate 5-HT2A receptors; antiviral via direct virucidal action on herpes simplex.
supplements.bestTime: 30–60 minutes before bed or stressful events
supplements.withFood: Can be taken with or without food

supplements.name: Wild Yam Extract (Dioscorea)
supplements.summary: A root herb containing diosgenin used as a precursor for synthetic progesterone production; used for menopausal symptoms, menstrual cramps, and osteoporosis prevention though body cannot convert diosgenin to progesterone directly.
supplements.shortDesc: Diosgenin-containing herb for menopausal and menstrual symptom relief.

forms.name: Wild Yam Cream (topical)
forms.notes: Topical form; diosgenin cannot convert to progesterone in body.

supplements.mechanism: Diosgenin binds to estrogen receptors weakly (phytoestrogen); anti-inflammatory via COX-2 inhibition; antispasmodic in GI and uterine smooth muscle; synthetic conversion to progesterone requires lab processing not possible in body.

supplements.name: Chlorophyll / Chlorophyllin
supplements.summary: The green pigment of plants and its water-soluble derivative chlorophyllin, used for deodorizing (bad breath, body odor), wound healing, potential anti-cancer properties, and antioxidant effects.
supplements.shortDesc: Plant pigment for odor control, wound healing, and antioxidant/anticancer properties.

forms.name: Chlorophyllin (sodium copper chlorophyllin)
forms.notes: Water-soluble derivative; more stable than chlorophyll.

forms.name: Liquid Chlorophyll (drops)
forms.notes: Popular in water drinks; lower potency.

supplements.mechanism: Chlorophyllin binds aflatoxin B1 and other carcinogens in GI tract reducing absorption; antioxidant via porphyrin ring similar to heme; accelerates wound healing via stimulation of fibroblast proliferation; odor control via binding amines and sulfur compounds.

supplements.name: Myo-Inositol
supplements.summary: The most bioavailable isomer of inositol, acting as a second messenger in insulin and FSH signaling, with strong evidence for PCOS, insulin resistance, anxiety (via serotonin/GABA modulation), and OCD treatment.
supplements.shortDesc: Inositol isomer for PCOS, insulin sensitivity, anxiety, and OCD.

forms.name: Myo-Inositol (powder)
forms.notes: Most studied form for PCOS and metabolic health.

forms.name: Myo-Inositol + D-Chiro-Inositol (40:1 ratio)
forms.notes: 40:1 ratio mirrors physiological plasma ratio; superior for PCOS.

supplements.mechanism: Second messenger for insulin receptor signaling; improves FSH signaling in ovarian follicles; precursor to phosphatidylinositol (PI3K pathway); modulates serotonin receptors; increases GABA sensitivity reducing anxiety.

supplements.name: D-Chiro-Inositol
supplements.summary: An inositol isomer that mediates insulin's intracellular signaling in muscle and liver tissue, particularly effective for PCOS, hyperandrogenism, and insulin resistance when combined with myo-inositol in a 40:1 ratio.
supplements.shortDesc: Inositol isomer for PCOS androgen reduction and insulin signaling in muscle/liver.

forms.name: D-Chiro-Inositol (pure)
forms.notes: Standalone form; often combined with myo-inositol.

supplements.mechanism: Mediates insulin's intracellular signal in muscle and liver via IPG (inositolphosphoglycan) messengers; reduces androgen synthesis in theca cells; reduces LH/FSH ratio improving ovarian function; activates pyruvate dehydrogenase complex.
sideEffects: Possible negative oocyte quality at very high doses

supplements.name: Magnolia Bark / Honokiol
supplements.summary: A bark extract containing honokiol and magnolol, potent anxiolytic and neuroprotective compounds that modulate GABA-A receptors and reduce cortisol with effects comparable to benzodiazepines without dependence.
supplements.shortDesc: Honokiol-rich bark for anxiety, cortisol reduction, and neuroprotection without dependence.
supplements.mechanism: Honokiol is a positive allosteric modulator of GABAA receptors (benzodiazepine-like without receptor downregulation); inhibits HPA axis activation reducing cortisol; magnolol inhibits NF-kB; both compounds cross BBB; honokiol induces mitochondrial apoptosis in cancer cells.
supplements.bestTime: 30–60 minutes before bed or stressful events
supplements.withFood: Can be taken with or without food

supplements.name: Kelp Extract (Fucoidan)
supplements.summary: A sulfated polysaccharide derived from brown seaweed with potent anti-cancer, antiviral, anticoagulant, and immune-modulating properties distinct from kelp's iodine content.
supplements.shortDesc: Fucoidan from brown seaweed for anti-cancer, antiviral, and immune modulation.

forms.name: Whole Kelp Powder
forms.notes: Broader mineral profile but less concentrated fucoidan.

supplements.mechanism: Fucoidan's sulfate groups mimic heparin → anticoagulant effects; inhibits selectin-mediated cancer metastasis; directly induces apoptosis in cancer cell lines; blocks viral attachment to host cell surface receptors; stimulates stromal-derived factor-1 for stem cell mobilization.
sideEffects: Thyroid disruption from excess iodine in whole kelp
Heavy metal accumulation in raw seaweed

supplements.name: N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC) Extended-Release
supplements.summary: An extended-release formulation of NAC maintaining steadier plasma cysteine levels for sustained glutathione replenishment, more effective for chronic oxidative stress, lung conditions, and PCOS compared to standard immediate-release NAC.
supplements.shortDesc: Extended-release NAC for sustained glutathione support and chronic conditions.

forms.name: NAC Extended-Release (600 mg ER)
forms.notes: Slower release maintains higher steady-state cysteine.

forms.name: NAC Immediate-Release
forms.notes: Higher peak levels useful for acute acetaminophen toxicity.

supplements.mechanism: Same as NAC: cysteine donor → glutathione synthesis; N-acetyl group provides mucolytic (breaks disulfide bonds in mucus); NF-kB inhibition reduces inflammation; sustained release provides more consistent cysteine supply for ongoing glutathione synthesis.

supplements.name: Digestive Bitters (Gentian/Dandelion Blend)
supplements.summary: Traditional herbal preparations containing bitter compounds (amarogentin, lactucin, taraxacin) that stimulate digestive secretions including bile, HCl, and pancreatic enzymes via the bitter taste receptor pathway.
supplements.shortDesc: Herbal bitters for digestive enzyme stimulation, bile flow, and HCl production.

forms.name: Digestive Bitters Tincture (gentian, dandelion, artichoke)
forms.notes: Most effective taken on tongue before meals.

forms.name: Bitter Herb Capsules
forms.bestFor: Convenient form; reduced bitter receptor activation
forms.notes: Less effective than tincture as bypasses taste receptors.

supplements.mechanism: Bitter compounds activate TAS2R (bitter taste receptors) on tongue and GI tract → vagal nerve stimulation → cephalic phase digestive response: HCl secretion, bile release, pancreatic enzyme release; gentian's amarogentin is most bitter compound known; dandelion increases bile volume.

supplements.name: Tributyrin (Butyrate Prodrug)
supplements.summary: A triglyceride form of butyric acid that delivers butyrate more efficiently than sodium butyrate to colonocytes; promotes gut barrier integrity, reduces inflammation, and supports microbiome health with better tolerability.
supplements.shortDesc: Triglyceride butyrate prodrug for superior gut barrier support and colonocyte fuel.

forms.name: Tributyrin (triglyceride)
forms.notes: Superior delivery to colon vs sodium butyrate; no butyric acid odor.

forms.name: Sodium Butyrate
forms.notes: Cheaper; strong odor; less efficient colon delivery.

supplements.mechanism: Tributyrin hydrolyzed in small intestine → butyrate → colonocyte beta-oxidation (primary energy source); butyrate inhibits histone deacetylases (HDAC) → epigenetic regulation of inflammatory genes; increases tight junction proteins (occludin, claudin-3); stimulates mucin production; reduces NF-kB.

supplements.name: Burdock Root
supplements.summary: A traditional root used as a gentle blood-purifying and skin-supporting herb with diuretic and antioxidant properties.
supplements.shortDesc: Traditional herb for skin and detox.

forms.name: Burdock Root Extract
forms.notes: Also used as a tea or tincture.

supplements.mechanism: Provides inulin and antioxidant polyphenols traditionally associated with skin and lymphatic support.

supplements.name: Goldenseal
supplements.summary: A berberine-containing herb traditionally used for immune support and gut and mucous-membrane health.
supplements.shortDesc: Berberine-rich herb for immunity and gut.
supplements.mechanism: Berberine and hydrastine provide antimicrobial and mucous-membrane-soothing activity.

supplements.name: Banaba Leaf
supplements.summary: A tropical leaf whose corosolic acid is studied for supporting healthy blood sugar and metabolism.
supplements.shortDesc: Corosolic-acid herb for blood sugar.

forms.name: Banaba Extract (1-2% corosolic acid)
forms.notes: Often combined with berberine or chromium.

supplements.mechanism: Corosolic acid promotes glucose uptake similarly to insulin signalling.

supplements.name: Hops
supplements.summary: The flower used in brewing, taken as an extract to promote relaxation and support sleep, often with valerian.
supplements.shortDesc: Calming flower for sleep, paired with valerian.
supplements.mechanism: Bitter acids may enhance GABAergic activity to promote sedation.

supplements.name: California Poppy
supplements.summary: A gentle calming herb traditionally used to ease anxiety and support restful sleep.
supplements.shortDesc: Gentle calming herb for sleep.

forms.name: California Poppy Extract
forms.notes: Often blended with other calming herbs.

supplements.mechanism: Alkaloids may interact with GABA and benzodiazepine pathways for mild sedation.

supplements.name: Kanna (Sceletium tortuosum)
supplements.summary: A South African succulent used to lift mood and ease stress, traditionally chewed and now standardised as Zembrin.
supplements.shortDesc: Mood-lifting, stress-easing succulent.

forms.name: Kanna Extract (Zembrin)
forms.notes: Standardised low-dose extract used in studies.

supplements.mechanism: Mesembrine acts as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor and PDE4 inhibitor.

supplements.name: Yohimbine
supplements.summary: A stimulant alkaloid from yohimbe bark used for fat loss and libido, but with notable cardiovascular and anxiety risks.
supplements.shortDesc: Stimulant alkaloid for fat loss (use caution).

forms.name: Yohimbine HCl
forms.notes: Start very low; not for those with anxiety or heart issues.

supplements.mechanism: Blocks alpha-2 adrenergic receptors, increasing adrenaline and fat mobilisation.

supplements.name: Cayenne (Capsaicin)
supplements.summary: A chili-pepper compound that may modestly boost metabolism, support circulation, and ease pain (topically).
supplements.shortDesc: Chili compound for metabolism and circulation.

forms.name: Capsaicin / Cayenne Extract
forms.notes: Take with food to limit GI burn.

supplements.mechanism: Activates TRPV1 receptors, raising thermogenesis and influencing pain signalling.

supplements.name: Peppermint Oil
supplements.summary: Enteric-coated peppermint oil has strong evidence for relieving IBS symptoms and digestive discomfort.
supplements.shortDesc: Eases IBS and digestive discomfort.
supplements.mechanism: Menthol relaxes intestinal smooth muscle via calcium channel modulation.

supplements.name: Fennel
supplements.summary: A carminative seed traditionally used to ease bloating, gas, and digestive cramping.
supplements.shortDesc: Soothes bloating and digestion.

forms.name: Fennel Seed Extract
forms.notes: Also used as a tea after meals.

supplements.mechanism: Anethole relaxes GI smooth muscle and has mild antispasmodic effects.

supplements.name: Guggul
supplements.summary: An Ayurvedic resin (Commiphora) traditionally used to support cholesterol balance and thyroid and metabolic health.
supplements.shortDesc: Ayurvedic resin for cholesterol and metabolism.
supplements.mechanism: Guggulsterones modulate bile acid receptors and lipid metabolism.

supplements.name: Arjuna
supplements.summary: An Ayurvedic bark (Terminalia arjuna) traditionally used as a cardiotonic to support heart function and circulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Ayurvedic bark for heart support.
supplements.mechanism: Provides antioxidant flavonoids and may support cardiac output and healthy blood pressure.

supplements.name: Bilberry
supplements.summary: A European berry rich in anthocyanins, traditionally used to support eye health and circulation.
supplements.shortDesc: Anthocyanin berry for eyes and circulation.
supplements.mechanism: Anthocyanins support capillary integrity and provide antioxidant protection.

supplements.name: Forskolin (Coleus forskohlii)
supplements.summary: A root compound that raises cyclic AMP, studied for body composition and hormonal signalling.
supplements.shortDesc: cAMP-raising herb studied for body composition.
supplements.mechanism: Activates adenylate cyclase, raising cAMP and downstream lipolytic signalling.

supplements.name: Yerba Mate
supplements.summary: A South American caffeinated tea providing energy, focus, and antioxidants with a smoother feel than coffee.
supplements.shortDesc: Caffeinated tea for energy and focus.
supplements.mechanism: Caffeine and theobromine boost alertness; polyphenols add antioxidant activity.

supplements.name: Guarana
supplements.summary: An Amazonian seed with a high, slow-release caffeine content used for energy and focus.
supplements.shortDesc: Slow-release natural caffeine source.

forms.name: Guarana Seed Extract
forms.notes: Caffeine releases more slowly than coffee.

supplements.mechanism: Delivers caffeine bound to tannins for a smoother, longer-lasting stimulant effect.

supplements.name: Oregon Grape
supplements.summary: A berberine-containing root (Berberis/Mahonia) traditionally used for skin conditions and digestion.
supplements.shortDesc: Berberine root for skin and digestion.

forms.name: Oregon Grape Root (berberine)
forms.notes: Topical creams also used for psoriasis.

supplements.mechanism: Berberine alkaloids provide antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activity.

supplements.name: Skullcap
supplements.summary: A calming herb (Scutellaria) traditionally used to ease anxiety and support relaxation and sleep.
supplements.shortDesc: Calming herb for anxiety and sleep.
supplements.mechanism: Flavonoids like baicalin may enhance GABA activity for relaxation.

supplements.name: Mullein
supplements.summary: A traditional respiratory herb used to soothe the airways and support lung and immune health.
supplements.shortDesc: Soothing herb for respiratory health.

forms.name: Mullein Leaf Extract
forms.notes: Commonly used as tea or tincture.

supplements.mechanism: Saponins and mucilage soothe mucous membranes and may loosen congestion.
sideEffects: Throat irritation from leaf hairs (strain tea)
